<a class="support-fab" href="tel:<?php echo h($supportPhone); ?>" aria-label="Call Customer Support 24/7">
    <span class="icon" aria-hidden="true"></span>
    <span>Call Support 24/7</span>
</a>

<footer>
    For immediate assistance, call us anytime at <a href="tel:<?php echo h($supportPhone); ?>"><?php echo h($supportPhone); ?></a>.
</footer>

<script>
(function(){
    'use strict';

    const qs = (sel, el = document) => el.querySelector(sel);
    const qsa = (sel, el = document) => Array.from(el.querySelectorAll(sel));
    const $searchForm = qs('#search-form');
    const $searchBtn = qs('#search-btn');
    const $searchError = qs('#search-error');
    const $results = qs('#results');
    const $resultsList = qs('#results-list');
    const $booking = qs('#booking');
    const $bookingForm = qs('#booking-form');
    const $bookingMsg = qs('#booking-msg');
    const $cancelBooking = qs('#cancel-booking');
    const $bookingDetails = qs('#booking-details');
    const $bookingSuccess = qs('#booking-success');
    const $bookingJson = qs('#booking-json');

    const CSRF_TOKEN = <?php echo json_encode($csrfToken, JSON_UNESCAPED_SLASHES | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE); ?>;

    function show(el) { el.style.display = ''; }
    function hide(el) { el.style.display = 'none'; }
    function setError(el, msg) { el.textContent = msg || ''; el.style.display = msg ? '' : 'none'; }

    function serializeForm(form) {
        const data = {};
        new FormData(form).forEach((v, k) => data[k] = v);
        return data;
    }

    function formatMoney(amount, currency) {
        try {
            return new Intl.NumberFormat(undefined, { style: 'currency', currency: currency || 'USD' }).format(amount);
        } catch (_) {
            return (currency || 'USD') + ' ' + amount;
        }
    }

    async function searchFlights(params) {
        const url = new URL(window.location.href);
        url.searchParams.set('action', 'search');
        Object.entries(params).forEach(([k, v]) => {
            if (v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== '') url.searchParams.set(k, v);
        });

        const res = await fetch(url.toString(), { method: 'GET', headers: { 'Accept': 'application/json' }, credentials: 'same-origin' });
        if (!res.ok) {
            const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
            throw new Error(err.error || `Search failed (HTTP ${res.status})`);
        }
        return res.json();
    }

    async function createBooking(payload) {
        const url = new URL(window.location.href);
        url.searchParams.set('action', 'book');

        const res = await fetch(url.toString(), {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'Accept': 'application/json',
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'X-CSRF-Token': CSRF_TOKEN,
            },
            credentials: 'same-origin',
            body: JSON.stringify(payload),
        });
        if (!res.ok) {
            const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
            throw new Error(err.error || `Booking failed (HTTP ${res.status})`);
        }
        return res.json();
    }

    async function getDetails(bookingId) {
        const url = new URL(window.location.href);
        url.searchParams.set('action', 'details');
        url.searchParams.set('booking_id', bookingId);
        const res = await fetch(url.toString(), { method: 'GET', headers: { 'Accept': 'application/json' }, credentials: 'same-origin' });
        if (!res.ok) {
            const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
            throw new Error(err.error || `Fetch details failed (HTTP ${res.status})`);
        }
        return res.json();
    }

    function renderResults(items) {
        $resultsList.innerHTML = '';
        if (!Array.isArray(items) || items.length === 0) {
            $resultsList.innerHTML = '<div class="muted">No flights found. Try different dates or airports.</div>';
            return;
        }

        items.forEach(item => {
            const id = String(item.id || '');
            const carrier = String(item.carrier || 'Airline');
            const flightNo = String(item.flight_number || '');
            const depart = String(item.departure_time || '');
            const arrive = String(item.arrival_time || '');
            const from = String(item.origin || '');
            const to = String(item.destination || '');
            const duration = String(item.duration || '');
            const price = Number(item.price?.amount || 0);
            const currency = String(item.price?.currency || 'USD');

            const row = document.createElement('div');
            row.className = 'item';
            row.innerHTML = `
                <div>
                    <div><strong>${carrier} ${flightNo}</strong> — ${from} → ${to}</div>
                    <div class="muted">${depart} → ${arrive} • ${duration}</div>
                </div>
                <div style="text-align:right;">
                    <div><strong>${formatMoney(price, currency)}</strong></div>
                    <button type="button" data-flight-id="${id}">Book</button>
                </div>
            `;
            const btn = row.querySelector('button');
            btn?.addEventListener('click', () => {
                // Pre-fill selected flight ID and show booking form
                qs('#selected_flight_id').value = id;
                hide($bookingDetails);
                hide($bookingMsg);
                show($booking);
                // Scroll to booking section
                $booking.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
            });
            $resultsList.appendChild(row);
        });
    }

    // Event: Search form submission
    $searchForm.addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        setError($searchError, '');
        hide($booking);
        hide($bookingDetails);
        show($results);
        $resultsList.innerHTML = '<div class="muted">Searching flights...</div>';
        $searchBtn.disabled = true;

        try {
            const data = serializeForm($searchForm);
            const payload = {
                origin: (data.origin || '').toString().toUpperCase(),
                destination: (data.destination || '').toString().toUpperCase(),
                passengers: parseInt(data.passengers, 10) || 1,
                depart_date: data.depart_date || '',
                return_date: data.return_date || '',
            };
            const res = await searchFlights(payload);
            renderResults(res.data);
        } catch (err) {
            setError($searchError, err.message || 'Search failed. Please try again.');
            hide($results);
        } finally {
            $searchBtn.disabled = false;
        }
    });

    // Event: Booking form submission
    $bookingForm.addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        setError($bookingMsg, '');
        hide($bookingDetails);

        const data = serializeForm($bookingForm);
        const flightId = data.flight_id || '';
        if (!flightId) {
            setError($bookingMsg, 'No flight selected.');
            return;
        }

        const payload = {
            flight_id: flightId,
            passenger: {
                name: data.name || '',
                email: data.email || '',
                phone: data.phone || '',
            },
        };

        qsa('button', $bookingForm).forEach(b => b.disabled = true);

        try {
            const res = await createBooking(payload);
            const booking = res.data || {};
            const bookingId = String(booking.id || '');
            $bookingSuccess.textContent = bookingId ? `Booking confirmed! ID: ${bookingId}` : 'Booking confirmed!';
            $bookingJson.textContent = JSON.stringify(booking, null, 2);
            show($bookingDetails);
            // Optionally fetch more detailed information:
            if (bookingId) {
                try {
                    const details = await getDetails(bookingId);
                    $bookingJson.textContent = JSON.stringify(details.data || booking, null, 2);
                } catch (_) {
                    // Ignore details fetch failure; we already have booking data.
                }
            }
            // Scroll to details
            $bookingDetails.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
        } catch (err) {
            setError($bookingMsg, err.message || 'Booking failed. Please try again.');
        } finally {
            qsa('button', $bookingForm).forEach(b => b.disabled = false);
        }
    });

    // Event: Cancel booking form
    $cancelBooking.addEventListener('click', () => {
        hide($booking);
    });

    // Set today's date as min for date inputs for better UX
    const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const departInput = qs('#depart_date');
    const returnInput = qs('#return_date');
    if (departInput) departInput.min = today;
    if (returnInput) returnInput.min = today;
    departInput?.addEventListener('change', () => {
        if (returnInput && departInput.value) {
            returnInput.min = departInput.value;
        }
    });
})();
</script>
</body>
</html>

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Scrape and summarize unique features of the token migration process from a target site.

This script fetches the page at newcontract-migrate.pages.dev (or a user-provided URL),
parses the HTML to identify sections likely describing migration features, and prints a
ranked list of unique features.

Key characteristics:
- Robust HTTP fetching with retries and timeouts
- Heuristic extraction of feature-like statements and bullet items
- Deduplication and scoring prioritizing migration-related content
- CLI options for JSON output, feature limits, logging verbosity, and custom URL

Usage:
  python3 migrate_features.py
  python3 migrate_features.py --url https://newcontract-migrate.pages.dev --json
  python3 migrate_features.py --max-features 12 --timeout 20 --verbose

Dependencies:

  - beautifulsoup4

"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import logging
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Iterable, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple
from urllib.parse import urlparse

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup, NavigableString, Tag
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter, Retry


DEFAULT_URL = "https://newcontract-migrate.pages.dev"
USER_AGENT = (
    "Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 "
    "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/119.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
)

# Keywords that hint a section or sentence is relevant
SECTION_KEYWORDS = [
    "feature",
    "benefit",
    "why",
    "migration",
    "migrate",
    "how it works",
    "overview",
    "token",
    "swap",
    "bridge",
    "claim",
    "airdrop",
    "process",
    "instructions",
    "guide",
    "faq",
    "security",
]
SENTENCE_KEYWORDS = [
    "migrat",  # captures migrate/migration
    "token",
    "fee",
    "gas",
    "claim",
    "deadline",
    "snapshot",
    "bridge",
    "swap",
    "airdrop",
    "burn",
    "mint",
    "contract",
    "audit",
    "kyc",
    "non-custod",
    "self-cust",
    "permissionless",
    "open-source",
    "on-chain",
    "multichain",
    "multi-chain",
    "l1",
    "l2",
    "merkle",
    "proof",
    "eligib",
    "allowlist",
    "whitelist",
    "vesting",
    "claim",
    "phase",
    "batch",
    "one-click",
    "zero",
    "immutable",
    "rollup",
    "safety",
    "revoke",
    "approval",
    "sign",
    "signature",
    "permit",
]


@dataclass
class FeatureItem:
    text: str
    score: float
    source_title: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class ExtractionResult:
    url: str
    extracted_at: str
    page_title: Optional[str]
    features: List[FeatureItem]
    section_titles: List[str]


def configure_logging(verbose: bool) -> None:
    """Configure root logger with simple format and appropriate level."""
    level = logging.DEBUG if verbose else logging.INFO
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=level,
        format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s",
    )


def create_session() -> requests.Session:
    """Create a requests session with retries, backoff, and user-agent."""
    session = requests.Session()
    retries = Retry(
        total=5,
        backoff_factor=0.8,
        status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504],
        allowed_methods=["HEAD", "GET", "OPTIONS"],
        raise_on_status=False,
        respect_retry_after_header=True,
    )
    adapter = HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retries, pool_connections=10, pool_maxsize=10)
    session.mount("http://", adapter)
    session.mount("https://", adapter)
    session.headers.update({"User-Agent": USER_AGENT, "Accept": "text/html, */*;q=0.8"})
    return session


def fetch_html(url: str, timeout: int = 15) -> str:
    """
    Fetch the HTML content for the given URL.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If URL is invalid or has unsupported scheme.
        requests.RequestException: On HTTP/connection errors.
    """
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    if parsed.scheme not in ("http", "https"):
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported URL scheme for '{url}'")
    if not parsed.netloc:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid URL '{url}'")

    session = create_session()
    logging.debug("Fetching URL: %s", url)
    resp = session.get(url, timeout=timeout)
    logging.debug("HTTP %s received", resp.status_code)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    content_type = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    if "text/html" not in content_type and "application/xhtml+xml" not in content_type:
        logging.warning("Unexpected content-type '%s'; proceeding to parse anyway.", content_type)
    return resp.text


def clean_soup(soup: BeautifulSoup) -> None:
    """Remove script/style/nav/footer elements to reduce noise."""
    for tag_name in ("script", "style", "noscript"):
        for t in soup.find_all(tag_name):
            t.decompose()
    for role_name in ("navigation", "banner", "contentinfo", "complementary"):
        for t in soup.find_all(attrs={"role": role_name}):
            t.decompose()
    for tag_name in ("nav", "footer", "iframe"):
        for t in soup.find_all(tag_name):
            t.decompose()


def normalize_whitespace(text: str) -> str:
    """Collapse excessive whitespace and trim."""
    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text or "").strip()


def is_relevant_text(text: str) -> bool:
    """Heuristic check: text is non-empty and contains relevant keywords."""
    t = text.lower()
    if len(t) < 8:
        return False
    return any(k in t for k in SENTENCE_KEYWORDS)


def score_sentence(text: str) -> float:
    """
    Score a sentence based on presence of prioritized terms and readability.

    Higher is better; negative scores will be filtered out by caller.
    """
    t = text.lower()
    score = 0.0
    # Strong signal: migration and token
    if "migrat" in t:
        score += 5.0
    if "token" in t:
        score += 3.0
    # Value propositions
    for kw, w in [
        ("unique", 2.0),
        ("feature", 1.5),
        ("benefit", 1.5),
        ("no ", 0.8),
        ("zero", 0.8),
        ("without", 0.7),
        ("non-custod", 1.2),
        ("self-cust", 1.2),
        ("permissionless", 1.0),
        ("audit", 1.0),
        ("security", 1.0),
        ("gas", 0.8),
        ("fee", 0.8),
        ("bridge", 0.6),
        ("snapshot", 0.9),
        ("deadline", 0.9),
        ("claim", 0.9),
        ("merkle", 0.9),
        ("proof", 0.5),
        ("on-chain", 0.7),
        ("open-source", 0.6),
        ("immutable", 0.6),
        ("approval", 0.6),
        ("revoke", 0.6),
        ("signature", 0.5),
        ("permit", 0.5),
    ]:
        if kw in t:
            score += w
    # Penalize excessive length
    if len(text) > 220:
        score -= 0.7
    if len(text) > 320:
        score -= 0.9
    return score


def extract_headings_within(el: Tag) -> List[str]:
    """Collect visible headings inside a container element."""
    headings = []
    for lvl in ("h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6"):
        for h in el.find_all(lvl):
            txt = normalize_whitespace(h.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True))
            if txt:
                headings.append(txt)
    return headings


def element_label(el: Tag) -> Optional[str]:
    """Derive a label/title for a section-like element."""
    # Prefer aria-label or data-title attributes
    for attr in ("aria-label", "data-title", "data-heading", "title"):
        v = el.get(attr)
        if v:
            return normalize_whitespace(v)
    # Next, nearest preceding heading
    prev = el.find_previous(["h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6"])
    if prev:
        return normalize_whitespace(prev.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True))
    # Or first heading inside
    headings = extract_headings_within(el)
    if headings:
        return headings[0]
    # Or class/id hint
    cid = el.get("id") or " ".join(el.get("class", []) or [])
    return normalize_whitespace(cid) if cid else None


def is_candidate_section(el: Tag) -> bool:
    """Heuristic: whether an element could be a section describing features."""
    if not isinstance(el, Tag):
        return False
    tag_name = el.name.lower()
    if tag_name not in ("section", "div", "article", "main", "aside"):
        return False
    # Check id/class hints
    id_class = " ".join([el.get("id", "")] + el.get("class", [])).lower()
    if any(k in id_class for k in SECTION_KEYWORDS):
        return True
    # Check headings text for hints
    for h in el.find_all(["h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6"]):
        txt = (h.get_text() or "").lower()
        if any(k in txt for k in SECTION_KEYWORDS):
            return True
    return False


def split_sentences(text: str) -> List[str]:
    """
    Split text into sentences or bullet-like chunks.
    Uses a simple regex; not locale-aware but adequate for web content.
    """
    # Normalize bullets and separators
    text = text.replace("•", ". ").replace("·", ". ").replace("–", " - ").replace("\u2013", " - ")
    # Split on periods, semicolons, newlines
    parts = re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s+|[\n\r;]+|\s*-\s+", text)
    sentences = [normalize_whitespace(p) for p in parts if normalize_whitespace(p)]
    return sentences


def extract_bullets(container: Tag) -> List[str]:
    """Extract bullet items from lists within a container."""
    items: List[str] = []
    for ul in container.find_all(["ul", "ol", "dl"]):
        # dt/dd appear in definition lists
        for li in ul.find_all(["li", "dt", "dd"], recursive=True):
            txt = normalize_whitespace(li.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True))
            if txt:
                items.append(txt)
    return items


def dedupe_preserve_order(items: Iterable[str]) -> List[str]:
    """Deduplicate text items while preserving original order."""
    seen = set()
    out: List[str] = []
    for it in items:
        key = it.lower()
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            out.append(it)
    return out


def extract_features_from_html(html: str, url: str) -> ExtractionResult:
    """
    Parse HTML to find and rank feature-like statements related to token migration.
    """
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    clean_soup(soup)

    page_title = None
    if soup.title and soup.title.string:
        page_title = normalize_whitespace(soup.title.string)

    # Gather candidate sections
    candidates: List[Tag] = []
    for el in soup.find_all(["section", "div", "article", "main", "aside"]):
        if is_candidate_section(el):
            candidates.append(el)

    # If no candidates found, fallback to the whole main or body
    if not candidates:
        main = soup.find("main") or soup.body or soup
        candidates = [main] if main else []

    section_titles: List[str] = []
    feature_items: List[FeatureItem] = []

    for section in candidates:
        label = element_label(section)
        if label:
            section_titles.append(label)

        # 1) Extract bullets first
        bullets = extract_bullets(section)
        for b in bullets:
            if is_relevant_text(b):
                feature_items.append(FeatureItem(text=b, score=score_sentence(b), source_title=label))

        # 2) Extract paragraphs and sentences
        for p in section.find_all(["p", "li", "span", "blockquote"]):
            raw = normalize_whitespace(p.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True))
            if not raw:
                continue
            for sent in split_sentences(raw):
                if is_relevant_text(sent):
                    feature_items.append(
                        FeatureItem(text=sent, score=score_sentence(sent), source_title=label)
                    )

    # If still very few items, broaden search across body text
    if len(feature_items) < 6:
        logging.debug("Broadening search due to low feature count (%d)", len(feature_items))
        body_text = normalize_whitespace(soup.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True))
        for sent in split_sentences(body_text):
            if is_relevant_text(sent):
                feature_items.append(
                    FeatureItem(text=sent, score=score_sentence(sent), source_title=None)
                )

    # Deduplicate by text
    deduped: List[FeatureItem] = []
    seen_texts = set()
    for item in sorted(feature_items, key=lambda i: i.score, reverse=True):
        key = item.text.lower()
        if key in seen_texts:
            continue
        seen_texts.add(key)
        deduped.append(item)

    # Filter out low-score noise
    filtered = [it for it in deduped if it.score > 0.4]

    # Final result
    return ExtractionResult(
        url=url,
        extracted_at=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        page_title=page_title,
        features=filtered,
        section_titles=dedupe_preserve_order(section_titles),
    )


def format_human_readable(result: ExtractionResult, max_features: int) -> str:
    """
    Produce a compact, human-readable summary list of features.
    """
    lines: List[str] = []
    title_line = f"Unique features of the token migration process ({result.url})"
    if result.page_title:
        title_line += f" — {result.page_title}"
    lines.append(title_line)
    lines.append("=" * len(title_line))

    if not result.features:
        lines.append("No migration-related features could be extracted automatically.")
        lines.append("Try increasing timeout or using the --verbose flag for debugging.")
        return "\n".join(lines)

    count = 0
    for item in result.features:
        if count >= max_features:
            break
        # Prefix with a bullet and include source section if available
        if item.source_title:
            lines.append(f"- {item.text} (source: {item.source_title})")
        else:
            lines.append(f"- {item.text}")
        count += 1

    return "\n".join(lines)


def result_to_json(result: ExtractionResult, max_features: int) -> str:
    """Serialize top features to JSON."""
    data = {
        "url": result.url,
        "page_title": result.page_title,
        "extracted_at": result.extracted_at,
        "section_titles": result.section_titles,
        "features": [
            {"text": fi.text, "score": round(fi.score, 3), "source_title": fi.source_title}
            for fi in result.features[:max_features]
        ],
    }
    return json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)


def parse_args(argv: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> argparse.Namespace:
    """Parse command-line arguments."""
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Extract unique migration features from a web page.",
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--url",
        default=DEFAULT_URL,
        help="Target URL to scrape",
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--timeout",
        type=int,
        default=15,
        help="HTTP request timeout in seconds",
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--max-features",
        type=int,
        default=10,
        help="Maximum number of features to display",
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--json",
        action="store_true",
        help="Output results as JSON instead of human-readable text",
    )
    p.add_argument(
        "--verbose",
        action="store_true",
        help="Enable verbose logging for debugging",
    )
    return p.parse_args(argv)


def main(argv: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> int:
    """Program entry point."""
    args = parse_args(argv)
    configure_logging(args.verbose)

    try:
        html = fetch_html(args.url, timeout=args.timeout)
    except ValueError as ve:
        logging.error("Invalid URL: %s", ve)
        return 2
    except requests.RequestException as re_err:
        logging.error("Network/HTTP error while fetching '%s': %s", args.url, re_err)
        return 3

    try:
        result = extract_features_from_html(html, args.url)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.exception("Failed to parse/extract features: %s", e)
        return 4

    if args.json:
        print(result_to_json(result, args.max_features))
    else:
        print(format_human_readable(result, args.max_features))

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

def _build_url(base_url: str, path: str, params: Optional[Mapping[str, Union[str, int, float, bool]]] = None) -> str:
    """

    If 'path' is an absolute URL (starts with http or https), it's used as-is.
    """
    if path.startswith("http://") or path.startswith("https://"):

    else:

        url = urljoin(base_url.rstrip("/") + "/", path.lstrip("/"))

    "please verify", "we sent", "activation", "subscribed", "subscription confirmed"
]

ERROR_TEXT_CANDIDATES = [
    "already", "invalid", "error", "failed", "try again", "not valid", "required"
]

COOKIE_ACCEPT_TEXTS = [
    "accept", "agree", "allow", "ok", "got it", "understood", "i agree", "i accept",
    "accept all", "allow all"
]

CAPTCHA_KEYWORDS = ["captcha", "recaptcha", "cf-challenge", "cloudflare"]


# ----------------------------- Utility Functions ---------------------------

def configure_logging(verbose: bool) -> None:
    """Configure application logging."""
    level = logging.DEBUG if verbose else logging.INFO
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=level,
        format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
        datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
    )


def is_valid_email(email: str) -> bool:
    """Basic email validation using a pragmatic regex."""
    pattern = r"^[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}$"
    return bool(re.match(pattern, email))


def normalize_base_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Ensure URL has a scheme and no trailing spaces."""
    url = url.strip()
    if not re.match(r"^https?://", url, flags=re.IGNORECASE):
        url = "https://" + url
    return url.rstrip("/")


def build_candidate_urls(base_url: str, candidates: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    """Create candidate full URLs to visit for discovering the registration form."""
    base = normalize_base_url(base_url)
    urls = []
    for path in candidates:
        path = path if path.startswith("/") else f"/{path}"
        urls.append(base + path)
    # Deduplicate while preserving order
    seen = set()
    unique_urls = []
    for u in urls:
        if u not in seen:
            unique_urls.append(u)
            seen.add(u)
    return unique_urls


def graceful_exit(signum, frame):
    """Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM gracefully."""
    logging.warning("Received termination signal. Exiting gracefully...")
    sys.exit(130)


# -------------------------- Page Interaction Helpers -----------------------

def try_accept_cookies(page: Page, timeout_ms: int = 4000) -> None:
    """
    Attempt to accept cookie banners if present by clicking on a visible accept-like button.
    Non-fatal if not found.
    """
    try:
        # Look for common cookie-related containers
        potential_banners = page.locator(
            "//*[contains(translate(text(),'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'cookie') "
            "or contains(translate(@aria-label,'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'cookie')]"
        )
        if potential_banners.count() > 0:
            # Search any visible button under these containers
            for text in COOKIE_ACCEPT_TEXTS:
                btn = page.locator(f"//button[normalize-space() and contains(translate(., 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'), '{text}')]")
                if btn.first.is_visible():
                    btn.first.click(timeout=timeout_ms)
                    logging.debug("Clicked cookie banner accept button.")
                    time.sleep(0.5)
                    return
    except PlaywrightTimeoutError:
        pass
    except Exception as e:
        logging.debug(f"Cookie banner handling skipped due to: {e!r}")


def detect_captcha_or_challenge(page: Page) -> bool:
    """Detect common CAPTCHA or bot-challenge hints on the page."""
    try:
        # Look for certain iframes or texts that hint at CAPTCHA
        # reCAPTCHA often uses iframe with title containing "captcha"
        captcha_iframe = page.locator("//iframe[contains(translate(@title,'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'captcha')]")
        if captcha_iframe.count() > 0 and captcha_iframe.first.is_visible():
            return True

        # Generic keywords anywhere in the page
        for kw in CAPTCHA_KEYWORDS:
            el = page.locator(
                f"//*[contains(translate(text(),'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'), '{kw.lower()}')]"
            )
            if el.count() > 0 and el.first.is_visible():
                return True
    except Exception:
        # Be conservative if errors occur while checking
        return False
    return False


def find_email_input_and_submit(page: Page) -> Optional[Tuple[Locator, Locator]]:
    """
    Attempt to find an email input and a submit button on the same form.
    Returns a tuple (email_input, submit_button) if found, else None.
    """
    # Candidate selectors for the email input
    email_input_selectors = [
        "input[type='email']",
        "input[placeholder*='email' i]",
        "input[name*='email' i]",
        "input[id*='email' i]",
    ]

    # Try to find visible email inputs
    for sel in email_input_selectors:
        inputs = page.locator(sel)
        count = inputs.count()
        for i in range(count):
            email_input = inputs.nth(i)
            if not email_input.is_visible():
                continue

            # Try to find ancestor form
            form = email_input.locator("xpath=ancestor::form[1]")
            if form.count() > 0 and form.first.is_visible():
                # Within the same form, find a submit button
                # Buttons with type submit or text candidates
                submit = form.locator("button[type='submit'], input[type='submit']")
                if submit.count() == 0:
                    # Try text-based buttons inside the form
                    for t in SUBMIT_TEXT_CANDIDATES:
                        submit = form.locator(
                            f"//form[.//{sel}]//button[normalize-space() and contains(translate(.,'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'{t}')]"
                        )
                        if submit.count() > 0:
                            break

                if submit.count() > 0:
                    submit_button = submit.first
                    if submit_button.is_visible():
                        logging.debug("Found email input and submit button within the same form.")
                        return email_input, submit_button

            # If no form ancestor, pick a nearby submit-like button within the same section
            for t in SUBMIT_TEXT_CANDIDATES:
                submit_button = email_input.locator(
                    f"xpath=ancestor::*[position()<=5]//button[normalize-space() and contains(translate(.,'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'{t}')]"
                )
                if submit_button.count() > 0 and submit_button.first.is_visible():
                    return email_input, submit_button.first

            # Fallback to any visible submit button on the page
            submit_button = page.locator("button[type='submit'], input[type='submit']")
            if submit_button.count() > 0 and submit_button.first.is_visible():
                return email_input, submit_button.first

    # Additionally, search for labels associated to email input
    label = page.locator("//label[contains(translate(.,'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'email')]")
    if label.count() > 0:
        # For first matching label, try 'for' attribute connection
        for i in range(label.count()):
            lab = label.nth(i)
            for_attr = lab.get_attribute("for")
            if for_attr:
                candidate_input = page.locator(f"#{for_attr}")
                if candidate_input.count() > 0 and candidate_input.first.is_visible():
                    # Find submit
                    submit_button = page.locator("button[type='submit'], input[type='submit']")
                    if submit_button.count() > 0 and submit_button.first.is_visible():
                        return candidate_input.first, submit_button.first

    return None


def try_check_required_checkboxes(container: Locator) -> None:
    """
    Attempt to check required checkboxes related to terms, privacy, or consent within the given container (e.g., form).
    Non-fatal if none found.
    """
    try:
        # Find checkboxes that look required by attribute or semantics
        checkbox = container.locator("input[type='checkbox']")
        num = checkbox.count()
        for i in range(num):
            cb = checkbox.nth(i)
            # Heuristics for required checkboxes
            attrs = {
                "required": cb.get_attribute("required"),
                "aria-required": cb.get_attribute("aria-required"),
                "name": (cb.get_attribute("name") or "").lower(),
                "id": (cb.get_attribute("id") or "").lower(),
            }
            label_text = ""
            try:
                # Find nearest label text
                lab = cb.locator("xpath=following::label[1]")
                if lab.count() > 0 and lab.first.is_visible():
                    label_text = (lab.first.inner_text() or "").lower()
            except Exception:
                pass

            looks_required = (
                attrs["required"] is not None or
                (attrs["aria-required"] or "").lower() in ("true", "1") or
                any(key in (attrs["name"] + " " + attrs["id"] + " " + label_text) for key in ["terms", "privacy", "policy", "consent", "agree"])
            )

            if looks_required:
                try:
                    cb.check(force=True)
                    logging.debug("Checked a required consent/terms checkbox.")
                except Exception:
                    # Some checkboxes are custom; try clicking the nearest label
                    try:
                        cb.evaluate("el => el.click()")
                        logging.debug("Clicked checkbox via JS.")
                    except Exception:
                        pass
    except Exception as e:
        logging.debug(f"Skipping checkbox handling due to: {e!r}")


def wait_for_feedback(page: Page, timeout_ms: int = 10000) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
    """
    Wait for a success or error message after form submission.
    Returns (is_success, message_text or None).
    """
    deadline = time.time() + (timeout_ms / 1000.0)
    while time.time() < deadline:
        # Check for obvious success text
        for text in SUCCESS_TEXT_CANDIDATES:
            loc = page.locator(f"//*[contains(translate(text(),'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'{text}')]")
            if loc.count() > 0 and loc.first.is_visible():
                try:
                    msg = loc.first.inner_text().strip()
                except Exception:
                    msg = text
                return True, msg

        # Check for obvious error text
        for text in ERROR_TEXT_CANDIDATES:
            loc = page.locator(f"//*[contains(translate(text(),'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ','abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'),'{text}')]")
            if loc.count() > 0 and loc.first.is_visible():
                try:
                    msg = loc.first.inner_text().strip()
                except Exception:
                    msg = text
                return False, msg

        # Sleep briefly and continue polling
        time.sleep(0.3)

    return False, None


# ----------------------------- Core Automation -----------------------------

def automate_registration(
    email: str,
    base_url: str,
    headless: bool,
    timeout_sec: int,
    allow_manual: bool,
    verbose: bool,
) -> int:
    """
    Core function that launches a browser, navigates to Sportify.Club, finds the registration form,
    fills and submits the email, and validates the result.

    Returns:
        0 on success, non-zero on failure.
    """
    urls = build_candidate_urls(base_url, DEFAULT_CANDIDATE_PATHS)
    logging.debug(f"Candidate URLs to inspect: {urls}")

    with sync_playwright() as p:
        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=headless)
        context = browser.new_context()
        page = context.new_page()
        page.set_default_timeout(timeout_sec * 1000)

        try:
            # Iterate candidate pages to find the form
            email_input = None
            submit_button = None
            found_url = None

            for url in urls:
                logging.info(f"Navigating to {url}")
                response = page.goto(url, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
                if response and not (200 <= response.status < 400):
                    logging.debug(f"Non-success HTTP status {response.status} at {url}")

                # Handle cookie banners to avoid overlay issues
                try_accept_cookies(page)

                if detect_captcha_or_challenge(page):
                    if allow_manual and not headless:
                        logging.warning("A CAPTCHA or bot challenge appears to be present. Please solve it in the browser window.")
                        input("Press Enter after solving the challenge to continue...")
                    else:
                        logging.error("Detected CAPTCHA or bot challenge. Run with --allow-manual and --headless=false to solve manually.")
                        return 3

                # Try to find the email form on this page
                res = find_email_input_and_submit(page)
                if res:
                    email_input, submit_button = res
                    found_url = url
                    logging.info(f"Found registration form on {url}")
                    break

            if not email_input or not submit_button:
                logging.error("Could not find an email registration form on the inspected pages.")
                return 4

            # Ensure field is interactable
            try:
                email_input.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
            except Exception:
                pass

            # Clear and type email
            try:
                email_input.fill("")  # Clear if supported
            except Exception:
                # Fallback to select-all delete
                try:
                    email_input.click()
                    page.keyboard.press("Control+A")
                    page.keyboard.press("Delete")
                except Exception:
                    pass

            email_input.fill(email, timeout=timeout_sec * 1000)
            logging.info(f"Entered email: {email}")

            # Attempt to check required consent/terms checkboxes in the same form or section
            try:
                form = email_input.locator("xpath=ancestor::form[1]")
                container = form.first if form.count() > 0 else page
                try_check_required_checkboxes(container)
            except Exception:
                pass

            # Submit the form
            try:
                submit_button.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
            except Exception:
                pass

            # Some forms require a tiny delay before submission
            time.sleep(0.3)
            submit_button.click(timeout=timeout_sec * 1000)
            logging.info("Submitted the registration form.")

            # After submit, wait for success/error feedback
            success, message = wait_for_feedback(page, timeout_ms=min(15000, timeout_sec * 1000))
            if success:
                logging.info(f"Registration appears successful. Message: {message or 'Success message detected.'}")
                return 0
            else:
                if message:
                    logging.warning(f"Registration may not have completed successfully. Detected message: {message}")
                else:
                    logging.warning("No definitive success message detected. The submission may still have been successful.")
                # Consider non-fatal, but return a distinct exit code to signal uncertainty
                return 1

        except PlaywrightTimeoutError as te:
            logging.error(f"Timeout while interacting with the page: {te}")
            return 5
        except Exception as e:
            logging.exception(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
            return 6
        finally:
            try:
                context.close()
                browser.close()
            except Exception:
                pass


# --------------------------------- CLI -------------------------------------

def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Automate free subscription registration on Sportify.Club."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--email",
        required=True,
        help="Email address to register.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        default=DEFAULT_BASE_URL,
        help=f"Base URL to target (default: {DEFAULT_BASE_URL}).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--headless",
        type=lambda v: str(v).lower() in ("true", "1", "yes", "y"),
        default=True,
        help="Run browser in headless mode (true/false). Default: true.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--timeout",
        type=int,
        default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
        help=f"Default operation timeout in seconds. Default: {DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--allow-manual",
        action="store_true",
        help="Allow manual intervention for CAPTCHAs or challenges (requires --headless=false).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--verbose",
        action="store_true",
        help="Enable verbose logging.",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def main() -> None:
    args = parse_args()
    configure_logging(args.verbose)

    # Register signal handlers for graceful termination
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, graceful_exit)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, graceful_exit)

    email = args.email.strip()
    if not is_valid_email(email):
        logging.error(f"Invalid email address: {email}")
        sys.exit(64)  # EX_USAGE

    if args.allow_manual and args.headless:
        logging.error("Manual intervention requires --headless=false.")
        sys.exit(64)

    exit_code = automate_registration(
        email=email,
        base_url=args.url,
        headless=args.headless,
        timeout_sec=max(5, args.timeout),
        allow_manual=args.allow_manual,
        verbose=args.verbose,
    )
    sys.exit(exit_code)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

prompts.prompt: Write a Python script that integrates with an API to retrieve product information for wire mesh machines and fences, as described on baochuanco.com.
prompts.generated_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Product Retriever for Wire Mesh Machines and Fences from baochuanco.com

This script integrates with common WordPress/WooCommerce REST APIs to retrieve product
information related to "wire mesh machines" and "fences" (and some localized Vietnamese
keywords). It is designed to be production-ready, with robust error handling, logging,
pagination support, retries, and flexible configuration.

Key Features:
- Auto-detects WooCommerce or falls back to WordPress REST endpoints.
- Supports WooCommerce authentication via environment variables or CLI flags.
- Filters by target keywords/categories (customizable).
- Outputs JSON or CSV.
- Resilient network handling with retry/backoff and timeouts.

Usage Examples:
  python fetch_products.py --base-url https://baochuanco.com --output products.json
  python fetch_products.py --base-url https://baochuanco.com --api-type woocommerce \
    --consumer-key $WC_CONSUMER_KEY --consumer-secret $WC_CONSUMER_SECRET

Environment Variables (optional):
  WC_CONSUMER_KEY
  WC_CONSUMER_SECRET
  API_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
  API_MAX_RETRIES
  API_BACKOFF_FACTOR

Note:
- Access to WooCommerce endpoints typically requires authentication.
- If WooCommerce auth is not available, the script will fall back to limited
  WordPress endpoints and keyword-based searches.

"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import dataclasses
import json
import logging
import os
import sys
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
from urllib.parse import urljoin, urlencode

import requests
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.util.retry import Retry


# ---------------------------
# Configuration and Constants
# ---------------------------

DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://baochuanco.com/"
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = float(os.getenv("API_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "15"))
DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES = int(os.getenv("API_MAX_RETRIES", "3"))
DEFAULT_BACKOFF_FACTOR = float(os.getenv("API_BACKOFF_FACTOR", "0.5"))
DEFAULT_UA = (
    "ProductRetriever/1.0 (+https://example.com; contact: ops@example.com)"
)

# Target keywords (English + Vietnamese synonyms) for wire mesh machines and fences.
DEFAULT_KEYWORDS = [
    # English
    "wire mesh machine",
    "mesh welding machine",
    "fence",
    "fences",
    "wire fence",
    "chain link fence",
    "gabion",
    "welded wire mesh",
    "barbed wire",
    "razor wire",

    # Vietnamese common terms (best-effort)
    "máy hàn lưới",
    "máy lưới",
    "lưới thép",
    "hàng rào",
    "lưới hàn",
    "lưới mắt cáo",
    "dây thép gai",
    "lưỡi bồ câu",
]

# Default categories or tags we try to match (best-effort)
DEFAULT_CATEGORY_HINTS = [
    "wire mesh",
    "fence",
    "máy",
    "lưới",
    "hàng rào",
]


# -------------
# Data Models
# -------------

@dataclass
class Image:
    src: str
    alt: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class Product:
    id: Union[int, str]
    name: str
    permalink: Optional[str] = None
    description: Optional[str] = None
    short_description: Optional[str] = None
    price: Optional[str] = None
    sku: Optional[str] = None
    categories: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    images: List[Image] = field(default_factory=list)
    raw: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Keep original payload


# -----------------
# Utility Functions
# -----------------

def setup_logger(verbose: bool = False) -> None:
    """Configure logging for the script."""
    level = logging.DEBUG if verbose else logging.INFO
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=level,
        format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
        handlers=[logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)],
    )


def make_session(
    max_retries: int = DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES,
    backoff_factor: float = DEFAULT_BACKOFF_FACTOR,
    timeout: float = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,
    user_agent: str = DEFAULT_UA,
) -> requests.Session:
    """
    Create a requests Session with retry and timeouts.

    Returns a tuple of (session, timeout) for convenience.
    """
    session = requests.Session()
    retry = Retry(
        total=max_retries,
        read=max_retries,
        connect=max_retries,
        backoff_factor=backoff_factor,
        status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504],
        allowed_methods=["GET", "POST"],
        raise_on_status=False,
    )
    adapter = HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry)
    session.mount("https://", adapter)
    session.mount("http://", adapter)
    session.headers.update({"User-Agent": user_agent})
    session.request_timeout = timeout  # custom attribute for our use
    return session


def safe_get(session: requests.Session, url: str, params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, auth=None) -> requests.Response:
    """
    GET with robust error handling and standardized timeout handling.
    """
    try:
        timeout = getattr(session, "request_timeout", DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)
        resp = session.get(url, params=params, timeout=timeout, auth=auth)
        return resp
    except requests.RequestException as exc:
        logging.error("Network error while GET %s: %s", url, exc)
        raise


def ensure_trailing_slash(url: str) -> str:
    """Ensure URL ends with a slash for reliable urljoin behavior."""
    return url if url.endswith("/") else url + "/"


def parse_bool_env(name: str, default: bool) -> bool:
    """Parse environment variable into boolean."""
    val = os.getenv(name)
    if val is None:
        return default
    return val.lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")


def normalize_text(s: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
    """Normalize text fields stripped of excessive whitespace."""
    if s is None:
        return s
    return " ".join(s.split())


def dedupe_preserve_order(items: Iterable[str]) -> List[str]:
    seen = set()
    out = []
    for x in items:
        if x not in seen:
            seen.add(x)
            out.append(x)
    return out


# ------------------------
# API Client Implementations
# ------------------------

class ApiClientBase:
    """
    Base API client interface for fetching products.
    """

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, session: Optional[requests.Session] = None):
        self.base_url = ensure_trailing_slash(base_url)
        self.session = session or make_session()

    def fetch_products(self, keywords: List[str]) -> List[Product]:
        raise NotImplementedError("Subclasses must implement fetch_products()")


class WooCommerceClient(ApiClientBase):
    """
    WooCommerce REST API client.

    Endpoints:
      - Products: /wp-json/wc/v3/products
      - Product Categories: /wp-json/wc/v3/products/categories

    Auth:
      - HTTP Basic Auth with consumer key and secret
      - OR via query parameters (?consumer_key=...&consumer_secret=...)
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        consumer_key: Optional[str] = None,
        consumer_secret: Optional[str] = None,
        session: Optional[requests.Session] = None,
        use_query_auth: bool = False,
    ):
        super().__init__(base_url, session=session)
        self.ck = consumer_key
        self.cs = consumer_secret
        self.use_query_auth = use_query_auth
        self.auth = None
        if self.ck and self.cs and not self.use_query_auth:
            self.auth = requests.auth.HTTPBasicAuth(self.ck, self.cs)

    def _endpoint(self, path: str) -> str:
        return urljoin(self.base_url, path.lstrip("/"))

    def _get(self, path: str, params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> requests.Response:
        url = self._endpoint(path)
        params = dict(params or {})
        if self.ck and self.cs and self.use_query_auth:
            params["consumer_key"] = self.ck
            params["consumer_secret"] = self.cs
        resp = safe_get(self.session, url, params=params, auth=self.auth)
        return resp

    def _paginate(self, path: str, params: Dict[str, Any], max_pages: int = 50) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Paginate through WooCommerce resources.
        """
        page = 1
        per_page = min(int(params.get("per_page", 100)), 100)
        results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

        while page <= max_pages:
            q = dict(params, page=page, per_page=per_page)
            resp = self._get(path, q)
            if resp.status_code == 401:
                raise PermissionError("Unauthorized (401) when accessing WooCommerce. Provide valid credentials.")
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                raise PermissionError("Forbidden (403) when accessing WooCommerce. Insufficient permissions.")
            if resp.status_code >= 400:
                raise RuntimeError(f"Error fetching WooCommerce {path}: HTTP {resp.status_code} - {resp.text[:300]}")
            batch = resp.json()
            if not isinstance(batch, list):
                raise ValueError(f"Unexpected response format for {path}: expected list")
            if not batch:
                break
            results.extend(batch)
            # If less than per_page returned, we've reached the end
            if len(batch) < per_page:
                break
            page += 1
        return results

    def fetch_products(self, keywords: List[str]) -> List[Product]:
        """
        Fetch products by performing keyword searches. If needed, could be extended to match categories.
        """
        # Attempt to fetch products for each keyword and deduplicate by ID
        products_by_id: Dict[Union[int, str], Product] = {}
        seen_ids: set = set()

        for kw in dedupe_preserve_order(keywords):
            params = {"search": kw, "status": "publish", "per_page": 100}
            logging.debug("WooCommerce search with keyword: %s", kw)
            try:
                items = self._paginate("/wp-json/wc/v3/products", params, max_pages=20)
            except PermissionError as e:
                # Propagate because WC typically needs auth; caller may handle fallback
                logging.warning("Permission error while fetching WooCommerce products: %s", e)
                raise
            except Exception as e:
                logging.error("Error during WooCommerce pagination: %s", e)
                continue

            for it in items:
                pid = it.get("id")
                if pid in seen_ids:
                    continue
                seen_ids.add(pid)
                prod = self._normalize_wc_product(it)
                products_by_id[pid] = prod

        return list(products_by_id.values())

    def _normalize_wc_product(self, it: Dict[str, Any]) -> Product:
        """
        Normalize WooCommerce product JSON into our Product model.
        """
        images = [
            Image(src=img.get("src"), alt=img.get("alt"))
            for img in it.get("images", []) if img.get("src")
        ]
        categories = [c.get("name") for c in it.get("categories", []) if c.get("name")]
        tags = [t.get("name") for t in it.get("tags", []) if t.get("name")]

        prod = Product(
            id=it.get("id"),
            name=normalize_text(it.get("name")),
            permalink=it.get("permalink"),
            description=normalize_text(it.get("description")),
            short_description=normalize_text(it.get("short_description")),
            price=str(it.get("price")) if it.get("price") is not None else None,
            sku=it.get("sku"),
            categories=categories,
            tags=tags,
            images=images,
            raw=it,
        )
        return prod


class WordPressClient(ApiClientBase):
    """
    WordPress REST API client.

    Tries to search for likely product-like content using posts, pages, and potential
    custom post types (e.g., 'product' if exposed without WooCommerce auth).
    """

    def _endpoint(self, path: str) -> str:
        return urljoin(self.base_url, path.lstrip("/"))

    def _get(self, path: str, params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> requests.Response:
        url = self._endpoint(path)
        resp = safe_get(self.session, url, params=params, auth=None)
        return resp

    def _paginate(self, path: str, params: Dict[str, Any], max_pages: int = 20) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        page = 1
        per_page = min(int(params.get("per_page", 100)), 100)
        results: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
        while page <= max_pages:
            q = dict(params, page=page, per_page=per_page)
            resp = self._get(path, q)
            if resp.status_code == 404:
                # Endpoint doesn't exist; break pagination for this path.
                break
            if resp.status_code >= 400:
                # Log and break to try other paths
                logging.debug("WordPress endpoint error on %s: %s", path, resp.text[:200])
                break
            try:
                batch = resp.json()
            except Exception as e:
                logging.debug("Invalid JSON from WP endpoint %s: %s", path, e)
                break
            if not isinstance(batch, list) or not batch:
                break
            results.extend(batch)
            if len(batch) < per_page:
                break
            page += 1
        return results

    def fetch_products(self, keywords: List[str]) -> List[Product]:
        """
        For WordPress without WooCommerce auth, search posts/pages/custom endpoints by keywords.
        """
        # Candidate endpoints to check in order of likelihood
        candidate_paths = [
            "/wp-json/wp/v2/product",
            "/wp-json/wp/v2/products",
            "/wp-json/wp/v2/posts",
            "/wp-json/wp/v2/pages",
        ]

        products: Dict[str, Product] = {}
        for kw in dedupe_preserve_order(keywords):
            for path in candidate_paths:
                logging.debug("WP search on %s with keyword: %s", path, kw)
                items = self._paginate(path, {"search": kw, "per_page": 100}, max_pages=10)
                for it in items:
                    prod = self._normalize_wp_item(it)
                    # Use permalink or ID as dedupe key
                    key = prod.permalink or str(prod.id)
                    if key not in products:
                        products[key] = prod

        return list(products.values())

    def _normalize_wp_item(self, it: Dict[str, Any]) -> Product:
        """
        Normalize WP post/page-like content.
        """
        # Attempt to extract fields from WordPress post/page structure
        pid = it.get("id") or it.get("ID") or it.get("slug")
        link = it.get("link") or it.get("guid", {}).get("rendered")
        title = None
        if isinstance(it.get("title"), dict):
            title = it["title"].get("rendered")
        elif isinstance(it.get("title"), str):
            title = it.get("title")

        content = None
        if isinstance(it.get("content"), dict):
            content = it["content"].get("rendered")
        elif isinstance(it.get("content"), str):
            content = it.get("content")

        excerpt = None
        if isinstance(it.get("excerpt"), dict):
            excerpt = it["excerpt"].get("rendered")
        elif isinstance(it.get("excerpt"), str):
            excerpt = it.get("excerpt")

        # WordPress REST often returns categories as IDs only without auth; names require additional calls.
        # We keep IDs for reference; names omitted to avoid extra API calls here.
        categories = it.get("categories") if isinstance(it.get("categories"), list) else []
        tags = it.get("tags") if isinstance(it.get("tags"), list) else []

        prod = Product(
            id=pid,
            name=normalize_text(title) or f"Item-{pid}",
            permalink=link,
            description=normalize_text(content),
            short_description=normalize_text(excerpt),
            price=None,
            sku=None,
            categories=[str(c) for c in categories],
            tags=[str(t) for t in tags],
            images=[],
            raw=it,
        )
        return prod


# -------------------------
# Discovery and Orchestration
# -------------------------

def discover_api_type(base_url: str, session: requests.Session) -> str:
    """
    Probe the site's REST index to determine if WooCommerce is available.
    Returns one of: "woocommerce", "wordpress"
    """
    index_url = urljoin(ensure_trailing_slash(base_url), "/wp-json")
    logging.info("Probing REST API at %s", index_url)
    try:
        resp = safe_get(session, index_url)
    except Exception:
        logging.warning("Could not reach REST API index; defaulting to WordPress client.")
        return "wordpress"

    if resp.status_code >= 400:
        logging.warning("REST index returned HTTP %s; defaulting to WordPress client.", resp.status_code)
        return "wordpress"

    try:
        data = resp.json()
    except Exception:
        logging.warning("REST index did not return valid JSON; defaulting to WordPress client.")
        return "wordpress"

    # Check available namespaces
    namespaces = data.get("namespaces", [])
    if "wc/v3" in namespaces or "wc/v2" in namespaces or "wc/v1" in namespaces:
        logging.info("Detected WooCommerce namespace.")
        return "woocommerce"

    logging.info("WooCommerce not detected; using WordPress client.")
    return "wordpress"


def filter_products_by_keywords(products: List[Product], keywords: List[str]) -> List[Product]:
    """
    Additional client-side filtering by keywords across name and description.
    """
    if not keywords:
        return products

    kw_lower = [k.lower() for k in keywords]

    def matches(p: Product) -> bool:
        text_fields = [
            p.name or "",
            p.short_description or "",
            p.description or "",
            " ".join(p.categories),
            " ".join(p.tags),
        ]
        blob = " ".join(text_fields).lower()
        return any(k in blob for k in kw_lower)

    return [p for p in products if matches(p)]


def serialize_products_json(products: List[Product]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """
    Convert list of Product objects into JSON-serializable dictionaries.
    """
    out: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    for p in products:
        out.append({
            "id": p.id,
            "name": p.name,
            "permalink": p.permalink,
            "description": p.description,
            "short_description": p.short_description,
            "price": p.price,
            "sku": p.sku,
            "categories": p.categories,
            "tags": p.tags,
            "images": [{"src": i.src, "alt": i.alt} for i in p.images],
        })
    return out


def write_output(products: List[Product], path: str) -> None:
    """
    Write products to JSON or CSV based on file extension.
    """
    ext = os.path.splitext(path)[1].lower()
    if ext in (".json", ""):
        data = serialize_products_json(products)
        with open(path or "products.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
        logging.info("Wrote %d products to %s", len(products), path or "products.json")
    elif ext == ".csv":
        fields = [
            "id",
            "name",
            "permalink",
            "description",
            "short_description",
            "price",
            "sku",
            "categories",
            "tags",
            "images",
        ]
        with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as f:
            writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fields)
            writer.writeheader()
            for p in products:
                writer.writerow({
                    "id": p.id,
                    "name": p.name,
                    "permalink": p.permalink,
                    "description": p.description,
                    "short_description": p.short_description,
                    "price": p.price,
                    "sku": p.sku,
                    "categories": "; ".join(p.categories),
                    "tags": "; ".join(p.tags),
                    "images": "; ".join([i.src for i in p.images]),
                })
        logging.info("Wrote %d products to %s", len(products), path)
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported output format: {ext}")


# -------------
# CLI and Main
# -------------

def parse_args(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Retrieve product information for wire mesh machines and fences from baochuanco.com via REST APIs."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--base-url",
        default=DEFAULT_BASE_URL,
        help="Base site URL (default: https://baochuanco.com/)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--api-type",
        choices=["auto", "woocommerce", "wordpress"],
        default="auto",
        help="Which API to use (default: auto detect).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--consumer-key",
        default=os.getenv("WC_CONSUMER_KEY"),
        help="WooCommerce consumer key (env: WC_CONSUMER_KEY).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--consumer-secret",
        default=os.getenv("WC_CONSUMER_SECRET"),
        help="WooCommerce consumer secret (env: WC_CONSUMER_SECRET).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use-query-auth",
        action="store_true",
        help="Send WooCommerce credentials as query parameters instead of basic auth.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--keywords",
        nargs="*",
        default=DEFAULT_KEYWORDS,
        help="Search keywords to target relevant products.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="products.json",
        help="Output file path (JSON or CSV). Default: products.json",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--timeout",
        type=float,
        default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,
        help=f"HTTP request timeout in seconds (default: {DEFAULT_TIMEOUT})",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max-retries",
        type=int,
        default=DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES,
        help=f"Max HTTP retries (default: {DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES})",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--backoff-factor",
        type=float,
        default=DEFAULT_BACKOFF_FACTOR,
        help=f"Retry backoff factor (default: {DEFAULT_BACKOFF_FACTOR})",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--verbose",
        action="store_true",
        help="Enable verbose logging",
    )
    return parser.parse_args(argv)


def main(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> int:
    args = parse_args(argv)
    setup_logger(verbose=args.verbose)

    session = make_session(
        max_retries=args.max_retries,
        backoff_factor=args.backoff_factor,
        timeout=args.timeout,
    )

    base_url = ensure_trailing_slash(args.base_url)

    # Auto-discover API type if needed
    if args.api_type == "auto":
        api_type = discover_api_type(base_url, session)
    else:
        api_type = args.api_type

    client: ApiClientBase

    # Select client based on type
    if api_type == "woocommerce":
        if not args.consumer_key or not args.consumer_secret:
            logging.warning(
                "WooCommerce selected but no credentials provided. Requests may fail with 401/403."
            )
        client = WooCommerceClient(
            base_url=base_url,
            consumer_key=args.consumer_key,
            consumer_secret=args.consumer_secret,
            session=session,
            use_query_auth=args.use_query_auth,
        )
    else:
        client = WordPressClient(base_url=base_url, session=session)

    # Fetch products with appropriate error handling and fallback
    products: List[Product] = []
    try:
        products = client.fetch_products(args.keywords)
        logging.info("Fetched %d raw items via %s", len(products), api_type)
    except PermissionError as e:
        logging.warning("Permission error with %s client: %s", api_type, e)
        if api_type == "woocommerce":
            logging.info("Falling back to WordPress client without WooCommerce authentication.")
            client = WordPressClient(base_url=base_url, session=session)
            products = client.fetch_products(args.keywords)
        else:
            raise
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error("Failed to fetch products via %s: %s", api_type, e)
        return 2

    # Client-side filtering to ensure relevance
    filtered = filter_products_by_keywords(products, args.keywords)
    logging.info("Retained %d products after keyword filtering", len(filtered))

    # Write output
    try:
        write_output(filtered, args.output)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error("Failed to write output: %s", e)
        return 3

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())
