        "- Explore categories/tags: Helper, Warehouse, Housekeeping, Retail, Kitchen",
        "- Check Latest/Recent jobs daily; look for Urgent/Immediate/Walk-in",
        "- Create job alerts/subscribe if available; register/submit CV if supported",
        "- Check Walk-in Interviews sections and plan attendance",
        "- Apply with a customized note; never pay fees; record every application",
        "",
        "4) Daily/Weekly application cadence",
        "- Daily: Apply to 5–10 relevant new roles",
        "- 2–3x/week: Re-check walk-ins and urgent roles; refresh profile if supported",
        "- Weekly: Update tracking sheet; refine keywords and CV",
        "",
        "5) Application tracking template",
        "- Columns: Date | Job Title | Company | Source (FreeJobsInDubai.com) | Link | Status | Follow-up Date | Notes",
        "- Follow up after 3–5 business days when contact info is provided",
        "",
        "6) Complementary channels",
        "- Company career pages, general job boards, LinkedIn (maintain caution; no payments)",
        "",
        "7) Interview preparation",
        "- Topics: physical tasks, safety basics, shifts/overtime, teamwork",
        "- Bring: CV copies, ID docs, references if any",
        "- Be ready for basic practical tests; dress neatly",
        "",
        "8) UAE-specific considerations",
        "- Never pay recruiters; verify employer details",
        "- Be transparent on visa status and joining availability",
        "- Clarify salary, overtime, benefits, and allowances",
        "",
        f"9) Weekly goals tied to {site}",
        "- 30–50 quality applications/week via the site",
        "- 2–3 walk-in interviews/week as available",
        "- 1 profile/CV refresh per week (if supported)",
        "",
        "10) Quick checklist",
        "- Tailored CV and short cover note ready",
        f"- Daily scan of {site} for new Helper roles, filtered for Dubai",
        "- Track applications and follow up on time",
        "- Prepare for walk-ins with documents and attire",
        "",
        "Helpful actions on FreeJobsInDubai.com:",
        "- Bookmark Helper search/category pages",
        "- Explore Walk-in Interviews and Urgent sections when present",
        "- Register/Submit CV and enable alerts if available",
        "",
        "Tip: Consistency wins. Apply daily, track results, and refine weekly.",
    ]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def generate_strategy(cfg: StrategyConfig) -> str:
    """
    Generate the job search strategy string based on configuration.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If an unsupported format is provided.
    """
    fmt = cfg.fmt.strip().lower()
    if fmt == "markdown":
        return _generate_markdown(cfg)
    if fmt == "text":
        return _generate_text(cfg)
    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported format: {cfg.fmt}. Use 'markdown' or 'text'.")


# ------------------------------- CLI and I/O -------------------------------- #

def _parse_args(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> argparse.Namespace:
    """Parse command-line arguments."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate a job search strategy for Helper jobs in Dubai using FreeJobsInDubai.com.",
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    )
    parser.add_argument("--role", type=str, default="Helper", help="Target role")
    parser.add_argument("--location", type=str, default="Dubai", help="Target location")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        dest="fmt",
        choices=["markdown", "text"],
        default="markdown",
        help="Output format",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="Output file path. If omitted, prints to stdout.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--links",
        dest="include_links",
        action="store_true",
        help="Include helpful links (e.g., site homepage).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--no-links",
        dest="include_links",
        action="store_false",
        help="Exclude links.",
    )
    parser.set_defaults(include_links=True)

    parser.add_argument(
        "--log-level",
        type=str,
        default="INFO",
        choices=["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"],
        help="Logging verbosity",
    )
    return parser.parse_args(argv)


def _configure_logging(level: str) -> None:
    """Configure logging for the CLI."""
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO),
        format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s",
    )


def _safe_write(path: str, content: str) -> None:
    """
    Safely write content to a file, creating directories as needed.

    Raises:
        OSError: If the file cannot be written.
    """
    directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
    if directory and not os.path.exists(directory):
        logging.debug("Creating directory: %s", directory)
        os.makedirs(directory, exist_ok=True)

    tmp_path = f"{path}.tmp"
    try:
        with open(tmp_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write(content)
        os.replace(tmp_path, path)
        logging.info("Wrote strategy to: %s", path)
    except OSError as exc:
        # Clean up temp file on failure
        try:
            if os.path.exists(tmp_path):
                os.remove(tmp_path)
        except OSError:
            pass
        raise OSError(f"Failed to write output file '{path}': {exc}") from exc


def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
    """CLI entry point."""
    args = _parse_args(argv)
    _configure_logging(args.log_level)

    cfg = StrategyConfig(
        role=args.role.strip() or "Helper",
        location=args.location.strip() or "Dubai",
        fmt=args.fmt,
        include_links=bool(args.include_links),
    )

    logging.debug("Generating strategy with config: %s", cfg)

    try:
        strategy = generate_strategy(cfg)
    except ValueError as e:
        logging.error(str(e))
        return 2
    except Exception as e:
        logging.exception("Unexpected error while generating strategy: %s", e)
        return 1

    if args.output:
        try:
            _safe_write(args.output, strategy)
        except OSError as e:
            logging.error(str(e))
            return 3
    else:
        print(strategy)

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

prompts.prompt: Write a Python script that utilizes an API to analyze and visualize the concepts from the "Agroglifos" project featured on coarco.org.
prompts.generated_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""
Agroglifos Concept Analyzer and Visualizer

This script uses the public WordPress REST API exposed by coarco.org
to discover and fetch the "Agroglifos" project content, performs keyword/concept
extraction, and produces visualizations.

Features:
- Uses the WordPress REST API (/wp-json/wp/v2/search) to find Agroglifos-related entries.
- Fetches the full content via the corresponding /posts or /pages REST endpoints.
- Extracts concepts (keyphrases) using YAKE if available, otherwise falls back to TF-IDF.
- Generates a bar chart and a word cloud of the top concepts (if the required packages are installed).
- Saves extracted concepts to a CSV file.
- Production-friendly: logging, error handling, command-line options, and sensible defaults.

Requirements (install as needed):
    pip install requests beautifulsoup4
    pip install yake           # Optional: better single-document keyword extraction
    pip install scikit-learn   # Optional: fallback TF-IDF keyword extraction
    pip install matplotlib     # Optional: bar chart
    pip install wordcloud      # Optional: word cloud

Usage:
    python agroglifos_analyzer.py --query "Agroglifos" --out-dir ./output

Environment variables:
    COARCO_API_BASE_URL  # Override the base API URL (defaults to https://coarco.org/wp-json/wp/v2)

Note:
- This script relies on the standard WordPress REST API endpoints and may adapt to other WP sites.
- Ensure outbound network connectivity is allowed.

Author: Your Name
License: MIT
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import dataclasses
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

# Optional dependencies (loaded lazily and handled gracefully)
try:
    import yake  # type: ignore
    _HAS_YAKE = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_YAKE = False

try:
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  # type: ignore
    _HAS_MPL = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_MPL = False

try:
    from wordcloud import WordCloud  # type: ignore
    _HAS_WORDCLOUD = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_WORDCLOUD = False

try:
    from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer  # type: ignore
    import numpy as np  # type: ignore
    _HAS_SKLEARN = True
except Exception:
    _HAS_SKLEARN = False


@dataclass
class Settings:
    """Configuration settings for the analyzer."""
    base_url: str = dataclasses.field(default_factory=lambda: os.getenv("COARCO_API_BASE_URL", "https://coarco.org/wp-json/wp/v2"))
    query: str = "Agroglifos"
    max_results: int = 5
    top_k: int = 20
    out_dir: str = "./output"
    language: str = "es"  # Language code hint for YAKE; 'es' (Spanish) by default
    timeout: int = 15
    verify_tls: bool = True


class CoarcoAPI:
    """Thin client for interacting with a WordPress REST API."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: int = 15, verify_tls: bool = True) -> None:
        if not base_url.endswith("/"):
            base_url += "/"
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.verify_tls = verify_tls
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            "Accept": "application/json",
            "User-Agent": "AgroglifosAnalyzer/1.0 (+https://coarco.org)"
        })

    def search(self, query: str, subtype: Optional[str] = None, per_page: int = 20) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Search the WordPress site for content matching a query.

        :param query: Search string, e.g., "Agroglifos"
        :param subtype: Optional content subtype ('post', 'page', custom types). 'any' is default on server.
        :param per_page: Max results per page (WP default up to 100).
        :return: List of search result objects with id, title, url, subtype, etc.
        """
        url = f"{self.base_url}/search"
        params = {
            "search": query,
            "per_page": min(max(per_page, 1), 100)
        }
        if subtype:
            params["subtype"] = subtype

        try:
            resp = self.session.get(url, params=params, timeout=self.timeout, verify=self.verify_tls)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            results = resp.json()
            if not isinstance(results, list):
                logging.warning("Unexpected search response format.")
                return []
            return results
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logging.error("HTTP error during search: %s", e)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            logging.error("Failed to parse JSON from search response.")
        return []

    def fetch_item(self, subtype: str, item_id: int) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """
        Fetch a specific content item by subtype and ID.

        :param subtype: 'post', 'page', or a custom post type.
        :param item_id: ID of the item returned by the search endpoint.
        :return: JSON object for the item or None.
        """
        # WordPress REST endpoints are pluralized: posts, pages, etc.
        endpoint = self._pluralize_subtype(subtype)
        url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint}/{item_id}"
        try:
            resp = self.session.get(url, timeout=self.timeout, verify=self.verify_tls)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp.json()
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logging.error("HTTP error fetching item %s/%s: %s", subtype, item_id, e)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            logging.error("Failed to parse JSON for item %s/%s", subtype, item_id)
        return None

    @staticmethod
    def _pluralize_subtype(subtype: str) -> str:
        """
        Convert a WP subtype to its REST collection endpoint.
        For known types we map directly; otherwise we fallback to naive pluralization.

        :param subtype: e.g., 'post', 'page', 'attachment', 'project'
        :return: endpoint name, e.g., 'posts', 'pages', 'attachments', 'projects'
        """
        mapping = {
            "post": "posts",
            "page": "pages",
            "attachment": "media",
        }
        if subtype in mapping:
            return mapping[subtype]
        # Naive pluralization fallback: 'project' -> 'projects'
        return f"{subtype}s"


def html_to_text(html: str) -> str:
    """
    Convert HTML to clean text using BeautifulSoup.
    Removes script/style and reduces excessive whitespace.

    :param html: HTML string.
    :return: Plain text.
    """
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html or "", "html.parser")
    for tag in soup(["script", "style", "noscript"]):
        tag.decompose()
    text = soup.get_text(separator=" ")
    # Normalize whitespace
    text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()
    return text


def filter_agroglifos_results(results: List[Dict], query: str) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Filter search results to emphasize entries about Agroglifos.

    :param results: List of WP search results.
    :param query: Query string used in search.
    :return: Filtered and prioritized list.
    """
    q_norm = query.strip().lower()
    prioritized: List[Tuple[int, Dict]] = []

    for r in results:
        title = (r.get("title") or "").strip()
        url = (r.get("url") or "").strip()
        subtype = (r.get("subtype") or "").strip()
        score = 0

        # Title relevance
        if q_norm in title.lower():
            score += 3
        # URL relevance (e.g., slug contains "agroglifos")
        if q_norm in url.lower():
            score += 3
        # Prefer non-attachment content
        if subtype in {"post", "page"}:
            score += 1

        prioritized.append((score, r))

    # Sort by descending score, then preserve original order
    prioritized.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
    return [r for _, r in prioritized if _is_valid_result(r)]


def _is_valid_result(result: Dict) -> bool:
    """Sanity-check a search result object."""
    return isinstance(result.get("id"), int) and isinstance(result.get("subtype"), str)


def extract_keywords_yake(text: str, language: str, top_k: int) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Extract keyphrases using YAKE.

    :param text: Document text.
    :param language: ISO language code hint (e.g., 'es', 'en').
    :param top_k: Number of top phrases to return.
    :return: List of (phrase, score) where lower score is better for YAKE.
    """
    if not _HAS_YAKE:
        raise RuntimeError("YAKE is not available.")
    # Configure YAKE; adjust max_ngram_size if longer phrases are expected
    kw_extractor = yake.KeywordExtractor(lan=language, n=3, top=top_k, dedupLim=0.9, features=None)
    keywords = kw_extractor.extract_keywords(text or "")
    # YAKE returns (phrase, score) with lower score being more relevant.
    # Normalize to ascending relevance by inverting sign for consistent plotting if needed later.
    return keywords


def extract_keywords_tfidf(texts: List[str], top_k: int, ngram_range: Tuple[int, int] = (1, 3)) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Extract top keyphrases using TF-IDF over a corpus.

    :param texts: One or more documents.
    :param top_k: Number of top phrases to return.
    :param ngram_range: N-gram range for tokenization.
    :return: List of (phrase, score) with higher score implying more relevance.
    """
    if not _HAS_SKLEARN:
        raise RuntimeError("scikit-learn is not available for TF-IDF extraction.")
    # Basic Spanish stopwords; extend as needed. Keeps dependencies light without downloading NLTK/stopwords.
    stopwords = {
        "el", "la", "los", "las", "un", "una", "unos", "unas", "de", "del", "y", "o", "u", "que", "en", "es", "por",
        "con", "para", "se", "al", "lo", "a", "como", "más", "menos", "su", "sus", "e", "si", "no", "sí", "ya", "muy",
        "sobre", "entre", "también", "pero", "sin", "este", "esta", "estos", "estas", "ese", "esa", "esos", "esas",
        "aquí", "allí", "allá", "debe", "deben", "fue", "son", "era", "eran", "ser", "está", "están", "estaba", "estaban",
        "hay", "hacer", "hacerlo", "hacerla", "cada", "cual", "cuales", "donde", "cuando", "cuándo", "dónde"
    }

    vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
        lowercase=True,
        analyzer="word",
        ngram_range=ngram_range,
        min_df=1,
        max_df=0.85,
        stop_words=stopwords
    )
    tfidf_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform(texts)
    # Aggregate TF-IDF scores across documents (sum)
    scores = tfidf_matrix.sum(axis=0).A1  # type: ignore
    terms = np.array(vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
    # Get top_k indices descending by score
    top_idx = scores.argsort()[::-1][:top_k]
    return [(terms[i], float(scores[i])) for i in top_idx if scores[i] > 0.0]


def normalize_yake_scores(pairs: List[Tuple[str, float]]) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Convert YAKE's "lower-is-better" scores into "higher-is-better" relevance for uniform plotting.

    :param pairs: List of (phrase, yake_score).
    :return: List of (phrase, normalized_score) with higher better.
    """
    if not pairs:
        return []
    scores = [s for _, s in pairs]
    max_s = max(scores)
    min_s = min(scores)
    # Avoid division by zero; map: normalized = 1 - (s - min) / (max - min)
    if max_s == min_s:
        return [(p, 1.0) for p, _ in pairs]
    return [(p, 1.0 - ((s - min_s) / (max_s - min_s))) for p, s in pairs]


def save_concepts_csv(concepts: List[Tuple[str, float]], out_path: str, method: str) -> None:
    """
    Save concepts to a CSV file.

    :param concepts: List of (phrase, score).
    :param out_path: Output CSV path.
    :param method: Extraction method label (e.g., 'yake', 'tfidf').
    """
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
    with open(out_path, "w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.writer(f)
        writer.writerow(["phrase", "score", "method"])
        for phrase, score in concepts:
            writer.writerow([phrase, f"{score:.6f}", method])


def plot_bar_chart(concepts: List[Tuple[str, float]], out_path: str, title: str) -> None:
    """
    Plot a horizontal bar chart of concept scores.

    :param concepts: List of (phrase, score) with higher being better.
    :param out_path: Output image path (png).
    :param title: Chart title.
    """
    if not _HAS_MPL:
        logging.warning("Matplotlib not available. Skipping bar chart.")
        return
    if not concepts:
        logging.warning("No concepts to plot.")
        return

    phrases = [p for p, _ in concepts][::-1]
    scores = [s for _, s in concepts][::-1]

    plt.figure(figsize=(10, max(4, len(concepts) * 0.4)))
    bars = plt.barh(range(len(concepts)), scores, color="#2a9d8f")
    plt.yticks(range(len(concepts)), phrases, fontsize=9)
    plt.xlabel("Relevance score")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.tight_layout()

    # Annotate bars with score values
    for rect, s in zip(bars, scores):
        width = rect.get_width()
        plt.text(width + max(scores) * 0.01, rect.get_y() + rect.get_height() / 2, f"{s:.3f}", va="center", fontsize=8)

    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
    plt.savefig(out_path, dpi=180)
    plt.close()
    logging.info("Saved bar chart: %s", out_path)


def generate_wordcloud(text: str, out_path: str) -> None:
    """
    Generate a word cloud from text.

    :param text: Source text.
    :param out_path: Output image path (png).
    """
    if not _HAS_WORDCLOUD:
        logging.warning("wordcloud not available. Skipping word cloud.")
        return
    if not text.strip():
        logging.warning("Empty text. Skipping word cloud.")
        return

    wc = WordCloud(
        width=1600,
        height=900,
        background_color="white",
        colormap="viridis"
    ).generate(text)

    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
    wc.to_file(out_path)
    logging.info("Saved word cloud: %s", out_path)


def select_texts_from_results(api: CoarcoAPI, results: List[Dict], max_items: int) -> List[Tuple[str, str, str]]:
    """
    For given search results, fetch full items and extract clean text.

    :return: List of tuples (title, text, url).
    """
    collected: List[Tuple[str, str, str]] = []
    for r in results[:max_items]:
        item_id = r.get("id")
        subtype = r.get("subtype")
        url = r.get("url") or ""
        if not isinstance(item_id, int) or not isinstance(subtype, str):
            continue

        item = api.fetch_item(subtype, item_id)
        if not item:
            continue

        title_html = (item.get("title") or {}).get("rendered", "")
        content_html = (item.get("content") or {}).get("rendered", "")
        title = html_to_text(title_html)
        text = html_to_text(content_html)

        # Skip empty content
        if not text.strip():
            continue

        collected.append((title, text, url))
    return collected


def analyze_concepts(texts: List[str], language_hint: str, top_k: int) -> Tuple[List[Tuple[str, float]], str]:
    """
    Analyze concepts using YAKE if available; fall back to TF-IDF.

    :param texts: List of textual documents.
    :param language_hint: Language code for YAKE.
    :param top_k: Number of concepts to extract.
    :return: (concepts, method) where concepts are (phrase, score) with scores normalized: higher is better.
    """
    combined_text = " ".join(texts).strip()
    if not combined_text:
        return [], "none"

    # Prefer YAKE for single-document keyphrase extraction
    if _HAS_YAKE:
        try:
            yake_pairs = extract_keywords_yake(combined_text, language_hint, top_k)
            normalized = normalize_yake_scores(yake_pairs)
            # Sort by normalized score descending and deduplicate phrases
            concepts = deduplicate_keep_order(sorted(normalized, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True), limit=top_k)
            return concepts, "yake"
        except Exception as e:
            logging.warning("YAKE extraction failed: %s. Falling back to TF-IDF.", e)

    # Fall back to TF-IDF if available
    if _HAS_SKLEARN:
        try:
            tfidf = extract_keywords_tfidf(texts if len(texts) > 1 else [combined_text], top_k)
            # Scores are already "higher is better"
            concepts = deduplicate_keep_order(tfidf, limit=top_k)
            return concepts, "tfidf"
        except Exception as e:
            logging.warning("TF-IDF extraction failed: %s.", e)

    # Minimal fallback: frequency-based naive approach
    logging.warning("Neither YAKE nor TF-IDF available. Using naive frequency method.")
    freq_concepts = naive_frequency_keywords(combined_text, top_k=top_k)
    return freq_concepts, "frequency"


def deduplicate_keep_order(pairs: Iterable[Tuple[str, float]], limit: int) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Deduplicate phrases (case-insensitive) while preserving order.

    :param pairs: Iterable of (phrase, score)
    :param limit: Maximum number to keep
    :return: Deduplicated list limited to 'limit'
    """
    seen = set()
    out: List[Tuple[str, float]] = []
    for phrase, score in pairs:
        key = phrase.strip().lower()
        if key and key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            out.append((phrase.strip(), score))
        if len(out) >= limit:
            break
    return out


def naive_frequency_keywords(text: str, top_k: int) -> List[Tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Very simple frequency-based keyword extraction (unigrams and bigrams).

    :param text: Input text
    :param top_k: Number of terms to return
    :return: List of (term, frequency) with higher being better
    """
    # Basic tokenization: words only
    tokens = [t.lower() for t in re.findall(r"[A-Za-zÁÉÍÓÚÜÑáéíóúüñ]+", text)]
    if not tokens:
        return []

    # Spanish stopwords subset
    stop = {
        "el", "la", "los", "las", "un", "una", "unos", "unas", "de", "del", "y", "o", "u", "que", "en", "es", "por",
        "con", "para", "se", "al", "lo", "a", "como", "más", "menos", "su", "sus", "e", "si", "no", "sí", "ya", "muy",
        "sobre", "entre", "también", "pero", "sin", "este", "esta", "estos", "estas", "ese", "esa", "esos", "esas",
        "aquí", "allí", "allá", "debe", "deben", "fue", "son", "era", "eran", "ser", "está", "están", "estaba",
        "estaban", "hay", "hacer", "cada", "cual", "donde", "cuando"
    }

    # Unigram frequencies
    from collections import Counter
    uni = Counter(t for t in tokens if t not in stop and len(t) > 2)

    # Bigram frequencies
    bigrams = Counter()
    for i in range(len(tokens) - 1):
        a, b = tokens[i], tokens[i + 1]
        if a in stop or b in stop:
            continue
        if len(a) <= 2 or len(b) <= 2:
            continue
        bigrams[f"{a} {b}"] += 1

    # Merge and select top_k
    combined = list(uni.items()) + list(bigrams.items())
    combined.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    return [(term, float(freq)) for term, freq in combined[:top_k]]


def configure_logging(verbosity: int) -> None:
    """Configure root logger based on verbosity level."""
    level = logging.WARNING
    if verbosity == 1:
        level = logging.INFO
    elif verbosity >= 2:
        level = logging.DEBUG
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=level,
        format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
        datefmt="%H:%M:%S"
    )


def parse_args(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> Settings:
    """Parse command-line arguments into Settings."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Analyze and visualize concepts from the 'Agroglifos' project on coarco.org using its REST API."
    )
    parser.add_argument("--base-url", default=os.getenv("COARCO_API_BASE_URL", "https://coarco.org/wp-json/wp/v2"),
                        help="Base WordPress REST API URL (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--query", default="Agroglifos", help="Search query to locate project content (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--max-results", type=int, default=5, help="Max number of matching items to analyze (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--top-k", type=int, default=20, help="Number of concepts to extract (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--out-dir", default="./output", help="Output directory for CSV and figures (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--language", default="es", help="Language hint for YAKE (default: %(default)s)")
    parser.add_argument("--no-verify-tls", action="store_true", help="Disable TLS certificate verification (not recommended)")
    parser.add_argument("-v", "--verbose", action="count", default=0, help="Increase logging verbosity (-v, -vv)")
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    configure_logging(args.verbose)
    return Settings(
        base_url=args.base_url,
        query=args.query,
        max_results=max(1, args.max_results),
        top_k=max(1, args.top_k),
        out_dir=args.out_dir,
        language=args.language,
        verify_tls=not args.no_verify_tls
    )


def main() -> int:
    """Entry point."""
    settings = parse_args()
    os.makedirs(settings.out_dir, exist_ok=True)

    logging.info("Using API base URL: %s", settings.base_url)
    api = CoarcoAPI(settings.base_url, timeout=settings.timeout, verify_tls=settings.verify_tls)

    # 1) Search for Agroglifos
    logging.info("Searching for '%s'...", settings.query)
    search_results = api.search(settings.query, per_page=max(20, settings.max_results))
    if not search_results:
        logging.error("No search results found for query '%s'.", settings.query)
        return 1

    filtered = filter_agroglifos_results(search_results, settings.query)
    if not filtered:
        logging.error("No valid Agroglifos-related results found.")
        return 1

    # 2) Fetch and clean content
    items = select_texts_from_results(api, filtered, settings.max_results)
    if not items:
        logging.error("No content could be fetched for the selected results.")
        return 1

    titles = [t for t, _, _ in items]
    texts = [x for _, x, _ in items]
    urls = [u for _, _, u in items]
    combined_text = "\n\n".join(texts)

    # 3) Analyze concepts
    concepts, method = analyze_concepts(texts, settings.language, settings.top_k)
    if not concepts:
        logging.error("Concept extraction failed or returned no results.")
        return 1

    # 4) Outputs
    # 4a) CSV
    csv_path = os.path.join(settings.out_dir, "agroglifos_concepts.csv")
    save_concepts_csv(concepts, csv_path, method)

    # 4b) Bar chart
    chart_path = os.path.join(settings.out_dir, "agroglifos_concepts_bar.png")
    plot_bar_chart(concepts, chart_path, title=f"Top {len(concepts)} concepts (method: {method})")

    # 4c) Word cloud (use combined text for better visuals)
    wc_path = os.path.join(settings.out_dir, "agroglifos_wordcloud.png")
    generate_wordcloud(combined_text, wc_path)

    # 5) Console summary
    print("Agroglifos Concept Analysis")
    print("===========================")
    print(f"Method: {method}")
    print("Analyzed items:")
    for i, (t, u) in enumerate(zip(titles, urls), 1):
        print(f"  {i}. {t} -> {u}")
    print("")
    print("Top concepts:")
    for i, (phrase, score) in enumerate(concepts, 1):
        print(f"  {i:2d}. {phrase}  ({score:.3f})")
    print("")
    print(f"Saved: {csv_path}")
    if _HAS_MPL:
        print(f"Saved: {chart_path}")
    if _HAS_WORDCLOUD:
        print(f"Saved: {wc_path}")

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        sys.exit(main())
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        print("\nInterrupted by user.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(130)

prompts.prompt: How can I use the momentum-trend analysis from 2xEther.com to create an API that evaluates the best trading position between ETHT and ETHD?
prompts.generated_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""
Production-ready FastAPI service to evaluate the best trading position (ETHT vs ETHD)
using momentum-trend analysis from 2xEther.com.

Features:
- Providers for momentum-trend data:
  - TwoXEther API provider (configurable via env)
  - TwoXEther HTML scraper (best-effort parsing)
  - Mock provider (deterministic fallback for testing)
- Evaluation logic:
  - Maps momentum trend to best position ETHT (bullish), ETHD (bearish), or HOLD (neutral/low conviction)
- Robust error handling, logging, timeouts, and simple retry
- Optional API key protection via X-API-Key header
- Well-structured and documented code

Dependencies (install via pip):
- fastapi==0.115.0
- uvicorn[standard]==0.30.6
- httpx==0.27.2
- pydantic==2.8.2
- python-dotenv==1.0.1
- beautifulsoup4==4.12.3

Run:
- Set environment variables as needed (see Settings below)
- Start server: uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
- Example request: curl 'http://localhost:8000/signal'
- With API key (if configured): curl -H 'X-API-Key: <your-key>' 'http://localhost:8000/signal'

Environment variables:
- API_HOST (default: 0.0.0.0)
- API_PORT (default: 8000)
- API_KEY (optional; if set, requests must include header X-API-Key)
- TWOXETHER_API_URL (optional; e.g., https://2xether.com/api/momentum-trend)
- TWOXETHER_SOURCE_URL (default: https://2xether.com/)
- PROVIDER (one of: mock, api, scrape; default: mock)
- HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default: 8)
- HTTP_MAX_RETRIES (default: 2)
- LOG_LEVEL (default: INFO)

Notes:
- Since 2xEther.com may not expose a stable public API, this service supports multiple providers.
  For production, set PROVIDER=api and TWOXETHER_API_URL to a working endpoint if available,
  or PROVIDER=scrape to parse the public page as a fallback. Otherwise, PROVIDER=mock is used.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import sys
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Tuple, Literal

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Header, HTTPException, Query, Request, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from pydantic import ValidationError as PydanticValidationError


# ----------------------------
# Logging Configuration
# ----------------------------

def configure_logging() -> logging.Logger:
    """
    Configure application-wide logging with console handler and structured format.
    """
    logger = logging.getLogger("etht-ethd-signal")
    log_level = os.getenv("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    logger.setLevel(getattr(logging, log_level, logging.INFO))

    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    formatter = logging.Formatter(
        fmt="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s",
        datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ",
    )
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)

    # Avoid adding multiple handlers if reloaded
    if not logger.handlers:
        logger.addHandler(handler)

    # Force UTC timestamps in logs
    logging.Formatter.converter = lambda *args: datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).timetuple()
    return logger


logger = configure_logging()


# ----------------------------
# Settings / Configuration
# ----------------------------

class Settings(BaseModel):
    """
    Application settings loaded from environment variables.
    """
    api_host: str = Field(default=os.getenv("API_HOST", "0.0.0.0"))
    api_port: int = Field(default=int(os.getenv("API_PORT", "8000")))
    api_key: Optional[str] = Field(default=os.getenv("API_KEY"))

    provider: Literal["mock", "api", "scrape"] = Field(default=os.getenv("PROVIDER", "mock"))

    twoxether_api_url: Optional[str] = Field(default=os.getenv("TWOXETHER_API_URL"))
    twoxether_source_url: str = Field(default=os.getenv("TWOXETHER_SOURCE_URL", "https://2xether.com/"))

    http_timeout_seconds: float = Field(default=float(os.getenv("HTTP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "8")))
    http_max_retries: int = Field(default=int(os.getenv("HTTP_MAX_RETRIES", "2")))

    class Config:
        extra = "ignore"


settings = Settings()


# ----------------------------
# Data Models
# ----------------------------

class Trend(str, Enum):
    bullish = "bullish"
    bearish = "bearish"
    neutral = "neutral"


class MomentumTrend(BaseModel):
    """
    Normalized structure describing momentum-trend analysis.
    momentum_score is normalized to range [-1, 1] where:
      -1 => strong bearish
       0 => neutral
      +1 => strong bullish
    """
    trend: Trend = Field(description="Directional trend classification.")
    momentum_score: float = Field(ge=-1.0, le=1.0, description="Normalized momentum score in [-1, 1].")
    as_of: datetime = Field(description="UTC timestamp of the analysis snapshot.")
    source: str = Field(description="Information source (URL or provider name).")


class Position(str, Enum):
    ETHT = "ETHT"   # 2x Ether Token (long)
    ETHD = "ETHD"   # 2x Ether Bear Token (inverse/short)
    HOLD = "HOLD"   # no position / neutrality


class EvaluationResult(BaseModel):
    """
    Final recommendation derived from momentum-trend analysis.
    """
    position: Position
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Confidence in the recommendation [0,1].")
    reason: str
    momentum: MomentumTrend


# ----------------------------
# Provider Abstraction
# ----------------------------

class MomentumTrendProvider(ABC):
    """
    Abstract provider to fetch momentum-trend data.
    Concrete implementations:
    - TwoXEtherAPIProvider: queries a JSON API (if available)
    - TwoXEtherScraperProvider: scrapes 2xEther HTML page (best-effort)
    - MockProvider: deterministic local signal (for testing/dev)
    """
    @abstractmethod
    async def get_momentum_trend(self) -> MomentumTrend:
        raise NotImplementedError


# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------

def clamp(value: float, min_value: float, max_value: float) -> float:
    return max(min_value, min(max_value, value))


def normalize_score(score: float, min_in: float = -100.0, max_in: float = 100.0) -> float:
    """
    Normalize arbitrary scored input to [-1, 1].
    If score is already in [-1, 1], it's returned clamped.
    """
    if -1.0 <= score <= 1.0:
        return clamp(score, -1.0, 1.0)
    # Linear normalization
    rng = max_in - min_in
    if rng == 0:
        return 0.0
    norm = (score - min_in) / rng * 2.0 - 1.0
    return clamp(norm, -1.0, 1.0)


async def http_get_json_with_retries(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str, retries: int, timeout: float
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Lightweight retry wrapper for GET returning JSON.
    """
    last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
    for attempt in range(retries + 1):
        try:
            resp = await client.get(url, timeout=timeout)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp.json()
        except Exception as exc:
            last_exc = exc
            backoff = 0.5 * (2 ** attempt)
            logger.warning("GET %s failed on attempt %d/%d: %s (backoff %.1fs)",
                           url, attempt + 1, retries + 1, repr(exc), backoff)
            await asyncio.sleep(backoff)
    assert last_exc is not None
    raise last_exc


async def http_get_text_with_retries(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str, retries: int, timeout: float
) -> str:
    """
    Lightweight retry wrapper for GET returning text.
    """
    last_exc: Optional[Exception] = None
    for attempt in range(retries + 1):
        try:
            resp = await client.get(url, timeout=timeout)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp.text
        except Exception as exc:
            last_exc = exc
            backoff = 0.5 * (2 ** attempt)
            logger.warning("GET %s failed on attempt %d/%d: %s (backoff %.1fs)",
                           url, attempt + 1, retries + 1, repr(exc), backoff)
            await asyncio.sleep(backoff)
    assert last_exc is not None
    raise last_exc


# ----------------------------
# Provider Implementations
# ----------------------------

class TwoXEtherAPIProvider(MomentumTrendProvider):
    """
    Provider that queries a JSON API endpoint for momentum-trend data.
    Expected (example) JSON structure (flexible):
    {
        "trend": "bullish" | "bearish" | "neutral",
        "momentum": 0.73,   # any numeric value; will be normalized to [-1, 1]
        "as_of": "2025-09-23T12:34:56Z"  # ISO8601
    }
    If structure differs, keys can be configured or handled below.
    """
    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient, api_url: str):
        if not api_url:
            raise ValueError("TwoXEtherAPIProvider requires a valid TWOXETHER_API_URL")
        self.client = client
        self.api_url = api_url

    async def get_momentum_trend(self) -> MomentumTrend:
        payload = await http_get_json_with_retries(
            client=self.client,
            url=self.api_url,
            retries=settings.http_max_retries,
            timeout=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
        )
        # Attempt to parse flexible payloads
        trend_raw = payload.get("trend") or payload.get("direction") or payload.get("signal")
        momentum_raw = payload.get("momentum") or payload.get("score") or payload.get("strength")
        as_of_raw = payload.get("as_of") or payload.get("timestamp") or payload.get("time")

        if not trend_raw or momentum_raw is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Unexpected API response schema from {self.api_url}: {payload}")

        # Normalize trend tokens
        trend_token = str(trend_raw).strip().lower()
        if trend_token.startswith("bull"):
            trend = Trend.bullish
        elif trend_token.startswith("bear"):
            trend = Trend.bearish
        else:
            trend = Trend.neutral

        # Normalize score
        try:
            score_val = float(momentum_raw)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            # Attempt to derive from label e.g., "strong_bullish"
            label = str(momentum_raw).lower()
            if "strong" in label:
                score_val = 1.0 if trend == Trend.bullish else -1.0
            elif "weak" in label or "mild" in label:
                score_val = 0.25 if trend == Trend.bullish else -0.25 if trend == Trend.bearish else 0.0
            else:
                score_val = 0.0

        norm_score = normalize_score(score_val)

        # Parse timestamp
        try:
            if as_of_raw:
                as_of = datetime.fromisoformat(str(as_of_raw).replace("Z", "+00:00")).astimezone(timezone.utc)
            else:
                as_of = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        except Exception:
            as_of = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        return MomentumTrend(
            trend=trend,
            momentum_score=norm_score,
            as_of=as_of,
            source=self.api_url,
        )


class TwoXEtherScraperProvider(MomentumTrendProvider):
    """
    Provider that scrapes the public 2xEther.com page as a best-effort fallback.
    It tries to discover tokens describing 'momentum' and 'trend'.
    Since HTML may change, this parser is resilient but not guaranteed.

    Heuristics:
    - Search for text patterns like 'Momentum', 'Trend', 'Signal'
    - Extract bullish/bearish/neutral signals and numerical/qualitative momentum
    - If a numeric value is not found, map qualitative adjectives to scores
    """
    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient, source_url: str):
        self.client = client
        self.source_url = source_url.rstrip("/")

    @staticmethod
    def _infer_trend(text: str) -> Trend:
        t = text.lower()
        if "bull" in t or "uptrend" in t or "positive" in t:
            return Trend.bullish
        if "bear" in t or "downtrend" in t or "negative" in t:
            return Trend.bearish
        return Trend.neutral

    @staticmethod
    def _infer_score(text: str) -> float:
        """
        Attempt to extract a number; otherwise map adjectives to a normalized score.
        """
        t = text.lower()

        # Extract explicit numeric (e.g., +0.65, -32.1, 73%)
        num_match = re.search(r"([-+]?(\d+(\.\d+)?|\.\d+))\s*%?", t)
        if num_match:
            val = float(num_match.group(1))
            # If percent found, map 0-100 to 0-1
            if "%" in t and abs(val) <= 100:
                val = val / 100.0
            return normalize_score(val)

        # Qualitative mapping
        score = 0.0
        if any(k in t for k in ["strong", "high", "robust"]):
            score = 0.8
        elif any(k in t for k in ["moderate", "medium"]):
            score = 0.5
        elif any(k in t for k in ["weak", "low", "soft"]):
            score = 0.2
        else:
            score = 0.0
        # Sign by trend hints
        if "bear" in t or "down" in t or "negative" in t:
            score *= -1
        return clamp(score, -1.0, 1.0)

    async def get_momentum_trend(self) -> MomentumTrend:
        html = await http_get_text_with_retries(
            client=self.client,
            url=self.source_url,
            retries=settings.http_max_retries,
            timeout=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
        )
        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")

        # Gather text blocks likely containing signals
        candidates: list[str] = []
        for tag in soup.find_all(True, text=True):
            content = (tag.get_text() or "").strip()
            if not content:
                continue
            lc = content.lower()
            if any(key in lc for key in ["momentum", "trend", "signal", "etht", "ethd"]):
                candidates.append(content)

        # Fallback to page title if nothing found
        if not candidates:
            title = soup.title.get_text().strip() if soup.title else "No title"
            candidates = [title]

        # Aggregate and infer
        combined = " | ".join(candidates[:20])
        trend = self._infer_trend(combined)
        score = self._infer_score(combined)

        return MomentumTrend(
            trend=trend,
            momentum_score=score,
            as_of=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            source=self.source_url,
        )


class MockProvider(MomentumTrendProvider):
    """
    Deterministic mock provider for development/testing.
    Signal oscillates over time for realism:
    - Uses current minute to produce a stable pseudo-random score.
    """
    def __init__(self, seed: Optional[int] = None):
        self.seed = seed or int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d"))

    @staticmethod
    def _hash_to_unit(n: int) -> float:
        # Convert int to pseudo-random float in [-1, 1]
        n = (n ^ 0x9E3779B9) * 0x85EBCA6B
        n = (n ^ (n >> 13)) & 0xFFFFFFFF
        unit = (n % 10000) / 5000.0 - 1.0
        return clamp(unit, -1.0, 1.0)

    async def get_momentum_trend(self) -> MomentumTrend:
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        minute = int(now.strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M"))
        raw = self._hash_to_unit(self.seed ^ minute)

        # Derive trend classification from score
        if raw > 0.1:
            trend = Trend.bullish
        elif raw < -0.1:
            trend = Trend.bearish
        else:
            trend = Trend.neutral

        return MomentumTrend(
            trend=trend,
            momentum_score=raw,
            as_of=now,
            source="mock",
        )


# ----------------------------
# Evaluation Logic
# ----------------------------

class PositionEvaluator:
    """
    Evaluates the best position (ETHT vs ETHD vs HOLD) given MomentumTrend.
    - If bullish and momentum is sufficiently strong, prefer ETHT.
    - If bearish and momentum is sufficiently strong, prefer ETHD.
    - If neutral or weak conviction, prefer HOLD.
    Thresholds can be tuned for risk tolerance.
    """
    def __init__(self, enter_threshold: float = 0.2, strong_threshold: float = 0.6):
        self.enter_threshold = clamp(enter_threshold, 0.0, 1.0)
        self.strong_threshold = clamp(strong_threshold, 0.0, 1.0)

    def evaluate(self, momentum: MomentumTrend) -> EvaluationResult:
        score = clamp(momentum.momentum_score, -1.0, 1.0)
        abs_score = abs(score)

        # Default to HOLD if conviction is weak or neutral trend
        if momentum.trend == Trend.neutral or abs_score < self.enter_threshold:
            return EvaluationResult(
                position=Position.HOLD,
                confidence=1.0 - abs(self.enter_threshold - abs_score),
                reason="Neutral or weak momentum; holding position reduces risk of whipsaw.",
                momentum=momentum,
            )

        # Directional mapping
        if momentum.trend == Trend.bullish and score > 0:
            confidence = clamp(0.5 + 0.5 * (abs_score), 0.0, 1.0)
            reason = (
                "Bullish momentum-trend favors ETHT (leveraged long). "
                f"Conviction {'strong' if abs_score >= self.strong_threshold else 'moderate'}."
            )
            return EvaluationResult(
                position=Position.ETHT,
                confidence=confidence,
                reason=reason,
                momentum=momentum,
            )

        if momentum.trend == Trend.bearish and score < 0:
            confidence = clamp(0.5 + 0.5 * (abs_score), 0.0, 1.0)
            reason = (
                "Bearish momentum-trend favors ETHD (leveraged short). "
                f"Conviction {'strong' if abs_score >= self.strong_threshold else 'moderate'}."
            )
            return EvaluationResult(
                position=Position.ETHD,
                confidence=confidence,
                reason=reason,
                momentum=momentum,
            )

        # If trend and score disagree (e.g., bullish but negative score), recommend HOLD
        return EvaluationResult(
            position=Position.HOLD,
            confidence=0.4,
            reason="Conflicting signals between trend direction and momentum magnitude; prefer HOLD.",
            momentum=momentum,
        )


# ----------------------------
# FastAPI App and Dependencies
# ----------------------------

app = FastAPI(
    title="ETHT-ETHD Momentum Trend Signal API",
    version="1.0.0",
    description="API that evaluates best trading position between ETHT and ETHD using 2xEther momentum-trend analysis.",
)


class ProviderChoice(str, Enum):
    mock = "mock"
    api = "api"
    scrape = "scrape"


class AppState(BaseModel):
    http_client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None


state = AppState()
evaluator = PositionEvaluator()


async def get_http_client() -> httpx.AsyncClient:
    """
    Provide a shared AsyncClient with sensible defaults.
    """
    if state.http_client is None:
        state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(
            headers={"User-Agent": "etht-ethd-signal/1.0 (+https://2xether.com)"},
            follow_redirects=True,
            verify=True,
            timeout=httpx.Timeout(
                connect=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
                read=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
                write=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
                pool=settings.http_timeout_seconds,
            ),
        )
    return state.http_client


def require_api_key(x_api_key: Optional[str] = Header(default=None, alias="X-API-Key")):
    """
    Simple header-based API key auth. If API_KEY is set in env, it's required.
    """
    if settings.api_key:
        if not x_api_key or x_api_key != settings.api_key:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Invalid or missing API key")
    return True


async def resolve_provider(
    preferred: Optional[ProviderChoice] = None,
    client: httpx.AsyncClient = Depends(get_http_client),
) -> MomentumTrendProvider:
    """
    Resolve provider based on query param or environment default.
    """
    choice = preferred or ProviderChoice(settings.provider)
    if choice == ProviderChoice.api:
        if not settings.twoxether_api_url:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                detail="TWOXETHER_API_URL is not configured for api provider",
            )
        return TwoXEtherAPIProvider(client=client, api_url=settings.twoxether_api_url)
    elif choice == ProviderChoice.scrape:
        return TwoXEtherScraperProvider(client=client, source_url=settings.twoxether_source_url)
    else:
        return MockProvider()


# ----------------------------
# Routes
# ----------------------------

@app.get("/health", tags=["system"])
async def health() -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Lightweight health check endpoint.
    """
    return {
        "status": "ok",
        "time": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "provider_default": settings.provider,
        "source_url": settings.twoxether_source_url,
    }


@app.get("/momentum", response_model=MomentumTrend, tags=["signal"])
async def get_momentum(
    provider: MomentumTrendProvider = Depends(resolve_provider),
    _auth: bool = Depends(require_api_key),
) -> MomentumTrend:
    """
    Fetch raw momentum-trend from the configured provider.
    Example:
      GET /momentum?preferred=api
      GET /momentum?preferred=scrape
      GET /momentum (uses default)
    """
    try:
        return await provider.get_momentum_trend()
    except (httpx.HTTPError, ValueError, PydanticValidationError) as exc:
        logger.error("Failed to fetch momentum: %s", repr(exc))
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_502_BAD_GATEWAY, detail="Upstream momentum provider failed")
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.exception("Unexpected error in /momentum: %s", repr(exc))
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, detail="Internal server error")


@app.get("/signal", response_model=EvaluationResult, tags=["signal"])
async def get_signal(
    request: Request,
    preferred: Optional[ProviderChoice] = Query(default=None, description="Override provider: mock|api|scrape"),
    provider: MomentumTrendProvider = Depends(resolve_provider),
    _auth: bool = Depends(require_api_key),
) -> EvaluationResult:
    """
    Evaluate and return the best trading position (ETHT vs ETHD) based on momentum-trend.
    """
    try:
        momentum = await provider.get_momentum_trend()
        result = evaluator.evaluate(momentum)
        return result
    except (httpx.HTTPError, ValueError, PydanticValidationError) as exc:
        logger.error("Failed to compute signal: %s", repr(exc))
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_502_BAD_GATEWAY, detail="Upstream momentum provider failed")
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.exception("Unexpected error in /signal: %s", repr(exc))
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, detail="Internal server error")


# ----------------------------
# Exception Handlers
# ----------------------------

@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException):
    """
    Uniform HTTP error responses with ISO timestamps.
    """
    payload = {
        "error": {
            "status_code": exc.status_code,
            "detail": exc.detail,
        },
        "time": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "path": str(request.url),
    }
    return JSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content=payload)


@app.on_event("shutdown")
async def on_shutdown():
    """
    Graceful shutdown: close HTTP client.
    """
    if state.http_client:
        try:
            await state.http_client.aclose()
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.warning("Failed to close HTTP client: %s", repr(exc))


# ----------------------------
# Local Dev Entry Point
# ----------------------------

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Optional: run the server directly with Python (not recommended for production).
    import uvicorn

    uvicorn.run(
        "app:app",
        host=settings.api_host,
        port=settings.api_port,
        reload=True,
        log_level=os.getenv("LOG_LEVEL", "info").lower(),
    )

prompts.prompt: Write a Python script that utilizes an API to fetch emergency services like hospitals and clinics, reflecting the services offered on SafeSteth.com.
prompts.generated_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Emergency Services Finder Script

This script queries public OpenStreetMap data via the Overpass API to find emergency-related
healthcare services (e.g., hospitals and clinics) near a given location, reflecting services
similar to those offered on SafeSteth.com.

Features:
- Search by latitude/longitude or by address (via Nominatim geocoding).
- Configurable service types (hospital, clinic by default).
- Radius control (in meters).
- Output results as JSON or CSV (to stdout or a file).
- Robust HTTP handling with retries and timeouts.
- Well-structured domain model and clean parsing of OSM tags.
- Logging for operational visibility.

Requirements:
- Python 3.8+
- requests
  Install: pip install requests

Note:
- Please use a descriptive User-Agent when calling Nominatim/Overpass to comply with usage policies.
  You can override the default via --user-agent.

Usage examples:
- By coordinates:
  python emergency_services.py --lat 40.7128 --lon -74.0060 --radius 5000 --format json --output nyc_services.json

- By address:
  python emergency_services.py --address "San Francisco, CA" --radius 3000 --types hospital clinic --format csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import dataclasses
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import sys
import time
from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple

try:
    import requests
    from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
    from urllib3.util.retry import Retry
except ImportError as exc:
    print("Error: The 'requests' package is required. Install it via 'pip install requests'.", file=sys.stderr)
    raise


# ----------------------------
# Configuration and Constants
# ----------------------------

DEFAULT_OVERPASS_ENDPOINT = os.getenv("OVERPASS_API_URL", "https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter")
DEFAULT_NOMINATIM_ENDPOINT = os.getenv("NOMINATIM_API_URL", "https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search")
DEFAULT_USER_AGENT = os.getenv(
    "HTTP_USER_AGENT",
    "SafeStethEmergencyServiceFinder/1.0 (+https://example.com/contact; sysadmin@example.com)"
)

# Default service types to search
DEFAULT_SERVICE_TYPES = ("hospital", "clinic")

# Valid service values to allow for filtering (extendable)
VALID_SERVICE_TYPES = {
    "hospital",
    "clinic",
    "doctors",
    "pharmacy",
    "urgent_care",  # Some OSM data may use healthcare=urgent_care
    "ambulance_station",
}

# HTTP Settings
HTTP_TIMEOUT = (10, 30)  # (connect timeout, read timeout)
MAX_RETRIES = 3
BACKOFF_FACTOR = 0.6


# ----------------------------
# Data Model
# ----------------------------

@dataclasses.dataclass
class EmergencyService:
    """
    Domain model for an emergency-related healthcare service parsed from OSM/Overpass.
    """
    osm_id: str
    name: Optional[str]
    service_type: Optional[str]
    address: Optional[str]
    phone: Optional[str]
    website: Optional[str]
    opening_hours: Optional[str]
    emergency: Optional[str]
    latitude: Optional[float]
    longitude: Optional[float]
    source: str = "overpass"

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return dataclasses.asdict(self)


# ----------------------------
# HTTP Utilities
# ----------------------------

def build_http_session(user_agent: str) -> requests.Session:
    """
    Build a requests.Session with retries, backoff, and custom User-Agent.
    """
    session = requests.Session()
    retry = Retry(
        total=MAX_RETRIES,
        backoff_factor=BACKOFF_FACTOR,
        status_forcelist=(429, 500, 502, 503, 504),
        allowed_methods=frozenset(["GET", "POST"]),
        raise_on_status=False,
    )
    adapter = HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry, pool_connections=10, pool_maxsize=10)
    session.mount("https://", adapter)
    session.mount("http://", adapter)
    session.headers.update({"User-Agent": user_agent})
    return session


# ----------------------------
# Geocoding (Nominatim)
# ----------------------------

def geocode_address(address: str, session: requests.Session, nominatim_endpoint: str) -> Tuple[float, float]:
    """
    Geocode an address to lat/lon using Nominatim.

    Raises:
        RuntimeError: If geocoding fails or returns no results.
    """
    params = {
        "q": address,
        "format": "jsonv2",
        "limit": 1,
        "addressdetails": 0,
    }
    resp = session.get(nominatim_endpoint, params=params, timeout=HTTP_TIMEOUT)
    if resp.status_code != 200:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Geocoding failed with HTTP {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}")

    try:
        data = resp.json()
    except ValueError as e:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to decode geocoding response JSON: {e}") from e

    if not isinstance(data, list) or not data:
        raise RuntimeError("No geocoding results found for the given address.")

    lat = float(data[0]["lat"])
    lon = float(data[0]["lon"])
    logging.info("Geocoded address to lat=%.6f lon=%.6f", lat, lon)
    return lat, lon


# ----------------------------
# Overpass Query Construction
# ----------------------------

def build_overpass_query(lat: float, lon: float, radius_m: int, service_types: Iterable[str]) -> str:
    """
    Build an Overpass QL query to fetch nodes/ways/relations for specified healthcare service types.

    Uses both 'amenity' and 'healthcare' keys to maximize coverage.
    """
    # Sanitize and join service types for regex
    types_regex = "|".join(sorted({re.escape(t) for t in service_types}))
    # Overpass QL query
    query = f"""
    [out:json][timeout:45];
    (
      node["amenity"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
      way["amenity"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
      relation["amenity"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
      node["healthcare"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
      way["healthcare"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
      relation["healthcare"~"^({types_regex})$"](around:{radius_m},{lat},{lon});
    );
    out center tags;
    """.strip()
    return query


# ----------------------------
# Overpass Client
# ----------------------------

def fetch_overpass_data(query: str, session: requests.Session, overpass_endpoint: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Execute an Overpass API query and return the parsed JSON.

    Raises:
        RuntimeError: on HTTP or parsing failure.
    """
    resp = session.post(overpass_endpoint, data={"data": query}, timeout=HTTP_TIMEOUT)
    if resp.status_code != 200:
        # Overpass sometimes returns 429 or 504; retries handled by session
        raise RuntimeError(f"Overpass API request failed with HTTP {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}")

    try:
        data = resp.json()
    except ValueError as e:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to decode Overpass JSON: {e}") from e

    if "elements" not in data or not isinstance(data["elements"], list):
        raise RuntimeError("Unexpected Overpass response format: 'elements' missing or invalid.")

    return data


# ----------------------------
# Parsing and Transformation
# ----------------------------

def build_address_from_tags(tags: Dict[str, str]) -> Optional[str]:
    """
    Construct a readable address from OSM tags if available.
    """
    if not tags:
        return None
    # Prefer a full address if present
    full = tags.get("addr:full")
    if full:
        return full

    parts = []
    housenumber = tags.get("addr:housenumber")
    street = tags.get("addr:street")
    city = tags.get("addr:city") or tags.get("addr:town") or tags.get("addr:village")
    state = tags.get("addr:state")
    postcode = tags.get("addr:postcode")

    street_line = " ".join(p for p in [housenumber, street] if p)
    locality_line = ", ".join(p for p in [city, state] if p)

    if street_line:
        parts.append(street_line)
    if locality_line:
        parts.append(locality_line)
    if postcode:
        parts.append(postcode)

    if parts:
        return ", ".join(parts)
    return None


def detect_service_type(tags: Dict[str, str]) -> Optional[str]:
    """
    Extract a normalized service type from tags, preferring amenity then healthcare.
    """
    if not tags:
        return None
    for key in ("amenity", "healthcare"):
        val = tags.get(key)
        if val:
            return val
    return None


def parse_overpass_elements(elements: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[EmergencyService]:
    """
    Parse Overpass JSON elements into a list of EmergencyService.
    """
    results: List[EmergencyService] = []

    for el in elements:
        el_type = el.get("type")  # node, way, relation
        osm_id = str(el.get("id"))
        tags = el.get("tags", {}) or {}

        # Coordinates: node has lat/lon; way/relation use 'center' if present
        lat = el.get("lat")
        lon = el.get("lon")
        center = el.get("center") or {}
        if lat is None or lon is None:
            lat = center.get("lat")
            lon = center.get("lon")

        service = EmergencyService(
            osm_id=f"{el_type}/{osm_id}" if el_type and osm_id else osm_id,
            name=tags.get("name"),
            service_type=detect_service_type(tags),
            address=build_address_from_tags(tags),
            phone=tags.get("phone") or tags.get("contact:phone"),
            website=tags.get("website") or tags.get("contact:website"),
            opening_hours=tags.get("opening_hours"),
            emergency=tags.get("emergency"),
            latitude=float(lat) if lat is not None else None,
            longitude=float(lon) if lon is not None else None,
        )
        results.append(service)

    return results


# ----------------------------
# Output Helpers
# ----------------------------

def output_json(services: List[EmergencyService], output_path: Optional[str]) -> None:
    """
    Output results as JSON to stdout or file.
    """
    payload = [s.to_dict() for s in services]
    if output_path:
        with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(payload, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    else:
        json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
        sys.stdout.write("\n")


def output_csv(services: List[EmergencyService], output_path: Optional[str]) -> None:
    """
    Output results as CSV to stdout or file.
    """
    fieldnames = [
        "osm_id",
        "name",
        "service_type",
        "address",
        "phone",
        "website",
        "opening_hours",
        "emergency",
        "latitude",
        "longitude",
        "source",
    ]
    if output_path:
        f = open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8", newline="")
        close_file = True
    else:
        f = sys.stdout
        close_file = False

    try:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, extrasaction="ignore")
        writer.writeheader()
        for s in services:
            writer.writerow(s.to_dict())
    finally:
        if close_file:
            f.close()


# ----------------------------
# Argument Parsing
# ----------------------------

def parse_args(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Fetch emergency-related healthcare services (e.g., hospitals, clinics) from OpenStreetMap via Overpass."
    )
    # Location input
    group = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
    group.add_argument("--address", type=str, help="Address or place to geocode (e.g., 'San Francisco, CA').")
    group.add_argument("--lat", type=float, help="Latitude for the search center.")
    parser.add_argument("--lon", type=float, help="Longitude for the search center (required if --lat is used).")

    # Search configuration
    parser.add_argument(
        "--radius",
        type=int,
        default=5000,
        help="Search radius in meters (default: 5000).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--types",
        nargs="+",
        default=list(DEFAULT_SERVICE_TYPES),
        help=f"Service types to include (default: {' '.join(DEFAULT_SERVICE_TYPES)}). Valid values include: {', '.join(sorted(VALID_SERVICE_TYPES))}.",
    )

    # Output
    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=["json", "csv"], default="json", help="Output format (default: json).")
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=str, default=None, help="Output file path (default: stdout).")

    # Endpoints and UA
    parser.add_argument("--overpass-url", type=str, default=DEFAULT_OVERPASS_ENDPOINT, help="Overpass API endpoint.")
    parser.add_argument("--nominatim-url", type=str, default=DEFAULT_NOMINATIM_ENDPOINT, help="Nominatim API endpoint.")
    parser.add_argument("--user-agent", type=str, default=DEFAULT_USER_AGENT, help="HTTP User-Agent header.")

    # Logging
    parser.add_argument("--verbose", action="store_true", help="Enable verbose logging.")

    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    # Validate lat/lon requirement
    if args.lat is not None and args.lon is None:
        parser.error("--lon is required when --lat is provided.")
    if args.lat is None and args.lon is not None:
        parser.error("--lat is required when --lon is provided.")

    # Validate radius
    if args.radius <= 0 or args.radius > 50000:
        parser.error("--radius must be in the range (0, 50000].")

    # Validate types
    normalized_types = []
    for t in args.types:
        t_norm = str(t).strip().lower()
        if not t_norm:
            continue
        # Allow custom types but warn if not in known set
        if t_norm not in VALID_SERVICE_TYPES:
            logging.warning("Type '%s' is not in known set; proceeding but results may vary.", t_norm)
        normalized_types.append(t_norm)
    if not normalized_types:
        parser.error("At least one service type must be provided.")
    args.types = normalized_types

    return args


# ----------------------------
# Main Execution
# ----------------------------

def main(argv: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> int:
    args = parse_args(argv)
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else logging.INFO,
        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    )

    session = build_http_session(args.user_agent)

    # Resolve coordinates
    if args.address:
        try:
            lat, lon = geocode_address(args.address, session, args.nominatim_url)
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error("Failed to geocode address: %s", e)
            return 2
        # Be polite to Nominatim (avoid hammering)
        time.sleep(1.0)
    else:
        lat = float(args.lat)
        lon = float(args.lon)
        logging.info("Using provided coordinates lat=%.6f lon=%.6f", lat, lon)

    logging.info(
        "Querying Overpass for types=%s within %dm of (%.6f, %.6f)",
        ",".join(args.types),
        args.radius,
        lat,
        lon,
    )

    query = build_overpass_query(lat, lon, args.radius, args.types)

    try:
        data = fetch_overpass_data(query, session, args.overpass_url)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error("Failed to fetch data from Overpass: %s", e)
        return 3

    elements = data.get("elements", [])
    services = parse_overpass_elements(elements)
    logging.info("Found %d services.", len(services))

    # Output
    try:
        if args.format == "json":
            output_json(services, args.output)
        else:
            output_csv(services, args.output)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error("Failed to write output: %s", e)
        return 4

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

"""
Outline the key points of military cooperation discussions between Mauritania and Morocco,
referencing the latest developments as mentioned on evela.info.

This script:
- Crawls evela.info's sitemaps (if available) and/or homepage to discover recent articles
- Retrieves and parses candidate articles
- Extracts sentences and summarizes key points related to Mauritania–Morocco military/defense/security cooperation
- Outputs a concise outline with citations (titles, dates, and URLs)

Key features:
- Production-minded: timeouts, retries, polite crawling with robots.txt compliance and rate limiting
- Robust HTML parsing with fallback strategies
- Minimal external dependencies (requests, beautifulsoup4)
- Clear logging and error handling
"""

)

)

prompts.generated_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Scrape and extract the features of the "LiteSpeed Web Server" as mentioned on blockhead.fun.

This script:
- Crawls pages on the target domain (default: https://blockhead.fun)
- Finds pages mentioning "LiteSpeed" or "LiteSpeed Web Server"
- Extracts nearby bullet points or clearly scoped list items that describe features
- Prints the deduplicated features in plain text or JSON

Requirements:
    pip install requests beautifulsoup4

Usage:
    python scrape_litespeed_features.py --base-url https://blockhead.fun --max-pages 50 --format text

Notes:
- The script respects robots.txt.
- Crawl scope is limited to the same domain.
- Heuristics are used to locate feature lists near relevant textual mentions.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import logging
import re
import time
from collections import deque
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Iterable, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
from urllib.parse import urljoin, urlparse

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup, Tag
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.util import Retry
import urllib.robotparser as robotparser


# ---------------------------
# Configuration and Constants
# ---------------------------

DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://blockhead.fun"
DEFAULT_MAX_PAGES = 50
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 10.0
DEFAULT_DELAY_SECONDS = 0.5

MENTION_KEYWORDS = [
    "litespeed web server",
    "litespeed",
    "lsws",
    "ls cache",
    "lscache",
]

# HTML tags considered as major sections for contextual extraction
SECTION_TAGS = {"section", "article", "main", "aside", "div", "body"}

# Domains commonly used for assets we should not crawl as pages
NON_HTML_CONTENT_TYPES = ("application/", "image/", "audio/", "video/", "font/", "text/css", "text/javascript")


@dataclass
class CrawlConfig:
    base_url: str = DEFAULT_BASE_URL
    max_pages: int = DEFAULT_MAX_PAGES

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""
Production-ready example of secure integration patterns for Evoke.cash API.

This module demonstrates best practices for:
- Credential management via environment variables (no hardcoded secrets)
- Request signing (HMAC) with timestamp and nonce to prevent tampering/replay
- Idempotency keys for safe retries of non-idempotent operations
- Strict timeouts, bounded retries with exponential backoff and jitter
- Secure logging with redaction of sensitive data
- Input/output validation and structured error handling
- Constant-time comparison for signature validation
- Webhook verification with timestamp drift checks
- Simple client-side rate limiting (token bucket)
- Minimal dependency footprint (standard library only)

Note:
- Adjust endpoints and header names to Evoke.cash API's official specification.
- For real deployments, consider certificate pinning and ASLR hardening mechanisms
  provided by your runtime, and secrets management via KMS/secret stores.
"""

import base64
import dataclasses
import datetime as dt
import hashlib
import hmac
import io
import json
import logging
import os
import random
import re
import secrets
import ssl
import sys
import threading

# ========================
# Configuration & Logging
# ========================

def _now_utc() -> dt.datetime:
    return dt.datetime.now(dt.timezone.utc)


class RedactingFilter(logging.Filter):
    """
    Redacts sensitive values from log records. This helps prevent secret leakage.
    """
    SENSITIVE_KEYS = {
        "api_key", "api_secret", "authorization", "password", "token",
        "x-evoke-signature", "x-evoke-webhook-signature", "x-evoke-secret"
    }

    def __init__(self, secrets_to_mask: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None):
        super().__init__()
        self._secrets = secrets_to_mask or {}

    @staticmethod
    def _redact_value(val: str) -> str:
        if not val:
            return val
        if len(val) <= 8:
            return "***REDACTED***"
        return f"{val[:4]}***REDACTED***{val[-4:]}"

    def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
        try:
            msg = record.getMessage()
            # Redact known header keys
            for key in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS:
                pattern = re.compile(rf"({key}\s*[:=]\s*)([^\s,;]+)", flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                msg = pattern.sub(r"\1***REDACTED***", msg)

            # Redact explicit secrets (e.g., api_key values)
            for name, secret_value in self._secrets.items():
                if not secret_value:
                    continue
                redacted = self._redact_value(secret_value)
                msg = msg.replace(secret_value, redacted)
            record.msg = msg
        except Exception:
            # In case of logging failure, never break the app
            pass
        return True


def setup_logger(secrets_to_mask: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> logging.Logger:
    logger = logging.getLogger("evoke_security")
    if logger.handlers:
        return logger  # Prevent duplicate handlers in repeated imports
    logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    fmt = logging.Formatter(
        "%(asctime)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s",
        datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ",
    )
    handler.setFormatter(fmt)
    handler.addFilter(RedactingFilter(secrets_to_mask))
    logger.addHandler(handler)
    return logger


# =====================================
# Settings (Environment-based, secure)
# =====================================

@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class Settings:
    """
    Loads settings from environment variables. Do not hardcode secrets.
    """
    base_url: str
    api_key: str
    api_secret: str
    webhook_secret: Optional[str] = None
    timeout_seconds: float = 10.0
    max_retries: int = 3
    backoff_factor: float = 0.5
    rate_limit_rps: float = 10.0  # client-side rate limiter

    @staticmethod
    def load_from_env() -> "Settings":
        base_url = os.getenv("EVOKE_BASE_URL", "https://api.evoke.cash")
        api_key = os.getenv("EVOKE_API_KEY")
        api_secret = os.getenv("EVOKE_API_SECRET")
        webhook_secret = os.getenv("EVOKE_WEBHOOK_SECRET")
        timeout_seconds = float(os.getenv("EVOKE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "10"))
        max_retries = int(os.getenv("EVOKE_MAX_RETRIES", "3"))
        backoff_factor = float(os.getenv("EVOKE_BACKOFF_FACTOR", "0.5"))
        rate_limit_rps = float(os.getenv("EVOKE_RATE_LIMIT_RPS", "10"))

        if not api_key or not api_secret:
            raise ValueError("Missing EVOKE_API_KEY or EVOKE_API_SECRET environment variables.")

        # Basic validation of base URL
        parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(base_url)
        if parsed.scheme not in ("https",):
            raise ValueError("Base URL must use HTTPS.")

        return Settings(
            base_url=base_url.rstrip("/"),
            api_key=api_key,
            api_secret=api_secret,
            webhook_secret=webhook_secret,
            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,
